question: All over the world, societies are facing growing problems with obesity. This problem affects children and adults. What are the reasons for this? and how could it be tackled.
essay:
Increasing obesity among youngesters is an issue faced by many communities around the globe. Eating an unhealthy food and lack of physical activities are some reasons behind this trend, but it can be tackled by educating people about importance of physical activities and banning or increasing tax on fast-food. 

The increasing trend of obesity is driven by consumption of unhealthy food and lack of excercise. Because junk food is more accessble and cheaper than healthy-food. Mnay people because of their busy schedules rely on convenience food and children are increasingly exposed to fast-food advertisements. Additionally, people spend long hours sitting on desk, watching television and using smartphones, which increases the number of calories intake thant he expenditure of energy, resulting in more gain weight. 

This problem can be solved by spreading awarness among people and through policies. Parents and schools should educate children about the benefits of nutritional food. And, government should put more tax on advertisements of sugery drinks to make them less affordable. Moreover, communities can open more sports centres open for all age group encourging healthy lifestyle. 

To conclude,  Obesity is increasing in many societies, but can be tackled by providing education and spreading awareness about significant of sports activities.

question: In recent year, online education is becoming more and more popular. What are the advantages and disadvantages of this phenomeneon? Use specific details and examples to explain your opinion.
essay:
These days,studying through online courses increased rapidly especially due to technological development and global events. While online offers several clear benefits, it also presents several drawbacks that cannot be ignored.

Flexible time schedule is the main advatage of studying virtual. Digital courses always have feature to choose the time of the lesson, meaning that if you are not able to go to the internet, you can change the time by informing your teacher 1-2 hours before the lesson. For instance, it is popular among young moms to use these kind od courses, because if they are stay late at work or have problems with baby they can switch the lesson for more comfortable time. As a result, lesson wont expire under any circumstanses

Unfortunetaly, there is no true learning atmospere in web-based classes. There are several factors which differ face-to-face study with virtual one. Student couldnt fully concentrate on subject while there is sometimes internet connection errors or advertisments pop-up. Also only modern computer devices can illustrate the image and voice of your tutor. There is a research conducted on efficency of training by computer and classroom learning where students were practised with the same teacher for 1 month. By the end of the month group which studied through face-to-face passed the test with hiegher results compared to those who studied online. 

In conclusion, although online based learning offers flexibility in terms of time, factors like internet connection or overpriced equipment makes face-to-face learning stiil alive.

question: Many men and women are making the decision to have children later in life. Why is this trend occurring? What are the impacts of this development on both family and society? Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own experience or knowledge.
essay:

For instance, a person who is pursuing a medical degree would often choose to complete the degree and focus on gaining relevant experience on becoming a doctor and achieve financial stability. However, on a societal level delaying parenthood can affect the population dynamics. This means that it could create economic challenges, such as increased pressure on healthcare system and pension schemes. A good example for this is that in Japan, delaying in children birth as often seen in increase of ageing population. This also has an adverse effect on the decreasing workforce and increasing dependency. Thus, delaying in having children can often have an adverse effect on the society. 

In conclusion, the decision to have children later in life stems from an educational, career and societal, bringing both advantages and challenges to both families and society. Balancing these factors is essential for both the individuals and social sustainability.

question: Nowadays more and more people have to compete with young people for the same jobs. What problems does this cause and what are the possible solutions?
essay:
These days many old people are competing with youngsters for similar work position. It causes increased unemployment and reduce organisational overall efficiency due to lack of less experienced workforce. But this problem can be solved by providing certain job reservation and trainings to elderly employees. 

First of all, many companies prefer to hire young people over old people, because they are willing to work at lower salaries and are more familiar with the new technology than their old counterparts. For example, in India, many aged individuals are facing challenges to find jobs after being replaced by young applicants. Moreover, this also leads to workplace tension, as young employee easily adapts to technology than old employee, and this generational gap leads to low productivity and efficiency. 

 

To tackle such kind of job-related problem, government and communities can take several measures. Like, government can set certain number of job position reserved for old people. So, they will not struggle to find a job. Additional, organisations should provide retraining programmes particularly designed for older people, to help them to adapt to new technology and work environment. For example, digital literacy course helps them to learn about new technological advancements. 

In conclusion, increasing competition among young and old people for same job is increasing unemployment rate in senior workers and reducing work productivity. But can be solved, if authorities and employers take suitable actions, like reserving job positions for old individuals and by offering training courses at organisational level.

question: recent years, many people have adopted a minimalist lifestyle, choosing to own only essential possessions. What are the advantages and disadvantages of this way of living?
essay:
From the remains of the late XX century modernist movement and a new global shift towards anti consumerism, a new “style” of living arose - minimalism. Coined by its simplicity and practicality, minimalism swept around the world becoming the new craze. So what made this lifestyle so popular and what may it lack?

On the advantageous part we have the anti consumerist worldview. If seeking to embrace practicality and efficiency, this trend provides the ideal blueprint. People can achieve better spending margins and utilize their space more efficiently, manufacturers can prioritize efficient mass production, even the living environment planners can embrace practicality - a win win situation for all.

However, on the contrary we see the "shallowness" of it all. Fundamentally, minimalism tends to lean towards a worldview which eliminates all emotional connection to the material world in favour of production efficiency. This eventually leads to the “great grey sludge”, where the environment begins to lose colour and beauty. Naturally,  it leads to great dissatisfaction for a person living in it.

Overall we can see the clear distinction between disadvantages and advantages of such a lifestyle. It becomes quite obvious that minimalism is not perfect and that we should be careful when creating the environment which surrounds us or it may lead us to unpleasant consequences.

question: Some believe that children should start school after the age of 7 while others think that school should begin from a very young age. Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
essay:
The charm of the Arctic is something that gets inside you and never leaves you. Subtle, elusive, but deep and persistent.

question: The prevention of health problems and illness is more important than treatment and medicine. Government funding should reflect this. To what extent do you agree
essay:
In the contemporary era, there is considerable debate regarding whether medical treatments and cures are more vital than preventative measures. Although taking initiatives steps at a very early stage can reduce the financial burden on the government, I contend that certain illnesses still require appropriate medication and treaments for a full recovery.

 

On the one hand, one of the prominent reasons why the prevention of health problems is crucial is that the health system is fully funded by authorities in some countries. Proactive measures are usually less expensive as compared to clinical treatment; therefore, investing in early intervention ensures that the government have to allocate its limited funds efficiently.

On the other hand, I contend that treatment and medicine are indispensable. This is because many medical conditions, such as genetic disorders, and sudden injuries from accidents cannot be recovered by a lifestyle change. Modern surgery and medications are the only way to ensure a fully recovery. For example, cancer is a disease, which neither can detect earlier nor recover from lifestyle changes. Such conditions require specialized treaments and medicines for a long term.

To conclude, even though proactive steps can reduce the financial requiements for authorities, I believe that some conditions cannot be solved unless proper treaments and medicines are not given to people.

question: What problems stop people from doing enough physical exercise and how can they be encouraged to be more active
essay:

One of the most significant problem is lack of time. Working long hours and academic pressure and household responsibilities leave little time and energy. Social media, gaming usually take individuals majority of time and this led to less physical activity.

Moreover, some people believe that cost of gym and they feel lack of motivation or awareness causes problems for doing exercises.

To address these issues, several effective measures can be taken. Both government and employers work together to solve these problems. Building parks, cycling tracks and outdoor gyms can be another solution. Employers should introduce short gym breaks or provide gym discount to promote active and healthy lifestyle. In addition to this, setting achievable goals and exercising with friends make workout more enjoyable. Doing these effective ways can be essential solutions to tackle with these problems.

In conclusion, lack of time, work pressure, modern technology always be problems to do of physical exercises. By making physical activity more enjoyable and accessible, individuals more likely to adopt healthier and more active lifestyle.

question: Do you think plastic packaging should be reduced in supermarkets? Why or why not?
essay: Plastic packaging has become a major environmental issue around the globe. Millions of tons of plastic waste are produced every year, with the majority ending up in landfills, rivers, and oceans. According to a report from the United Nations Environment Programme (2023), plastic pollution is now one of the biggest environmental challenges, posing risks to ecosystems, wildlife, and human health. Brazil is among the leading producers of plastic waste in Latin America. The Brazilian Association of Public Cleaning and Special Waste Companies (2023) found that the country generates millions of tons of solid waste each year, including a significant portion made up of plastic packaging. While Brazil has designed recycling programs and has environmental laws, its recycling system isn’t very efficient. Many cities lack proper waste collection and recycling systems, which leads to improper disposal of large amounts of plastic packaging. As a result, plastic waste pollution has become a serious environmental challenge in both urban and coastal areas.

question: Globalisation has both advantages and disadvantages. Discuss both and give your opinion.
essay:
Globalisation has become a defining feature of the modern world, influencing economies and cultures across the globe. While this development offers several benefits, it also presents significant drawbacks. This essay will examine both perspectives before presenting my own view.

One major advantage of globalisation is the expansion of international trade. Businesses are now able to access global markets, which increases economic growth and creates employment opportunities. As companies expand beyond national borders, consumers benefit from a wider variety of products at competitive prices. For example, multinational technology companies distribute devices worldwide, making advanced technology accessible to millions of people.

However, globalisation also has several negative consequences. One significant drawback is the erosion of local cultures. As global media and multinational corporations spread similar products and lifestyles around the world, traditional customs and languages can gradually disappear. This cultural homogenisation may weaken national identities and reduce cultural diversity.

In conclusion, although globalisations provides many advantages and promotes economic development and international cooperation, it can also threaten cultural diversity and create inequality. Overall, I believe that the benefits outweigh the disadvantages provided that governments implement policies to protect local communities.

question: In many countries around the world, rural people are moving to cities, so the population in the countryside is decreasing. Do you think this is a positive or a negative development?
essay:
There is an increasingly prevalent trend worldwide of people moving away from the country side to live in bigger cites. In my opinion this is a very worrying phenomenon, as it causes problems within agriculture and affects the life quality of people. 

Agricultural production is the first and foremost pillar of the supply chain of alimentation. Generally speaking, both crops and farmlands are located in the countyside. With people moving away, there is a major dip in available workforce within agriculture, causing problems in food production. A great example of this is the increasing price of corn in many first-world countries. What once used to be a crop grown entirely locally is now largely imported, showing the toll of relocation on agriculture. 

Looking at the problem from an other angle, we can see that many major cities now experience overpopluation. It is evident that with an increase in population density, the quality of life declines rapidly. As cities are more crowded, there is less space for green areas, residential zones and people-centric architecture. Considering Cairo, one of the world's most densly populated cities, we can clearly see the lack of sustainable architecture and dedicaded spaces for leasure and well being. 

In conclusion, this process of city-centric relocation and shifts in the poputaion density of certain areas, creates major problems within the agricultural supply chain and affects the quality of life of people directly. Therefore, it is very important to take steps in regulating or offseting this trend in innovative ways.

question: In some countries, more and more people are becoming interested in finding out about the history of the house or building they live in. What are the reasons for this? How can people research this?
essay:
The  renowned American Historian, Daniel J. Boorstin, has once said: “The greatest obstacle to discovery is not ignorance but the illusion of knowledge.” This is a quote that has deeply affected the way I see the world, and the way I perceive most essentially what we call “knowledge”. Perhaps, what Daniel J. Boorstin means when he says the illusion of knowledge is that it refers to the fact that we as human beings have adopted this false narrative that we do know everything, even when we actually don’t. Hence, let me make one thing clear for you; in today's world, you in fact don’t know anything… This is the paradox of the contemporary world. We live in a digital age, which makes us feel extremely lucky due to the fact that we have access to a vast amount of information with the rising digitalization. Now, finding information is just one button away from you. Thus, this is where the problem arises, not only access has become easier, but also the democratisation of information has taken place. In a sense, digital media has enabled the public to not only obtain but also to produce and circulate content. Hence, that is a very immense problem we fail to comprehend, but I will make it easy for you by simply saying; trust has become extinct. 

      The absence of trust is mainly due to false information and narratives that we are being exposed to on a daily basis. Now, if you think about it for a second, there is a huge surplus of information and plus these are not all coming from authorized and reliable sources. Therefore, this matter of trust is very complicated and is an invisible crisis that we as human beings go through. When being subject to these false information, we start building our reality upon false narratives, living our life based on false beliefs, norms, and values. If you believe these are personal matters; you are entirely mistaken. Such a thing happening makes the world more complicated not only an individual’s life, because we have to keep in mind that what is personal is very much political and public as well. Presumably, misinformation brings many risks within itself; like polarization, poor decisions made by the public, erosion of democracy and manipulation of people regarding elections, public health and even different types of crises. Therefore, one thing I know for sure is that we should be taking the issue seriously and combating it with a cautious approach. 

My personal view of a cautious approach is one that demands transparency in platforms, a more powerful media accountability and increased digital literacy among people.

question: Many companies are implementing remote work policies permanently. What are the potential long-term effects of this shift on urban development and community engagement? Is this trend ultimately beneficial?
essay:

Overusing technology can make a person's vision and posture worse. For instance, majority of remote workers have to be on the phone for 6-8 hours, which is too much for a human's eyes or brain.

Moreover, there is no communication between workers, as they are forced to spend a day with their computers only. It can lead to a person feeling aloneness and anxiety, because communication with others is crucial in shaping a person's mental health.

However, i stand by the view that it is still a beneficial change for companies. In conclusion, i believe there are more advantages than disadvantages. Working remotely causes problems, but it's benefits outweigh themand that's what matters. Overall, i think remote jobs are convenient and it's good that they are becoming a trend, when technologies nowadays allow it.

question: many countries, primary and secondary schools close for two months or more in the summer holidays. What is the value of long school holidays? What are the arguments in favour of shorter school holidays?
essay:
In many parts of the word, primary and secondary students are given summer breaks that last for two months or even longer. While extended holidays provide important benefits for student's development and well being, there are also convincing reasons why some people advocate shorter breaks. Long holidays allow students to rest and pursue personal growth, whereas shorter vacations may help maintain academic continuity and reduce social inequality.

One major value of long school holidays is that they give students sufficient time to recover from academic pressure. During the school year, children often face demanding schedulessss filled with lessons .assignments and examinations. An extended break enables them to relax mentally and physically, preventing burnout and improving long term motivation. For instance, students who participate in summer activities such as travelling, sports camps or creative workshops often return to school feeling refreshed and more angaged in their studies. 

However, supporter of shorter vacations argue that long breaks can disrupt learning progress. When students are away from academic study for several months, they may forget previously learned material, particularly in subjects that require continuous practice such as mathematics or languages. For example, teachers frequently report that the first few weeks after summer are spent reviewing old content, which reduces time available for new lessons and may slow overall academic advancement.

In conclusion, extended summer vacations offer valuable opportunities for rest, personal development and family bonding.

question: Many people around the world are choosing move to live in cities. What problems do people experience in big cities? Should governments encourage people to move to smaller towns?

For example, when people in the same area really need a new phone but there are fewer phones being produced, they would have to pay more for the phone.

question: Nowadays,many people choose to be self employed rather than work for a company or organization. Why might this be the case? What could be the disadvantages.
essay: Nowadays, many people decide to work for themselves for flexibility reason; they want to have a job that allows them to work on their own timeline, instead of having it being set by a certain company. This kind of flexibility, although beneficial for their own satisfaction, can have several disadvantages: first, work delays. A lot of people who prioritise flexibility might not be disciplined enough to finish their day-to-day tasks on the desired time, leaving more tasks for the next day. Second, reduced work-related social skills. Being self-employed means people do not have the advantage of meeting coworkers. This results in a reduced work-related social skills as they are not being exposed to working on acase with other people. Third, reduced chance of connecting unique individuals. Working with coworkers allows people to discuss various topics, whether it is work-related or personal topics, which can add value to their lives. Self-employment isolates this kind of fulfilling activity. Fourth, being self-employed can impact people's mental health. Self-employment removes contact hours with coworkers, and working without them can lead to loneliness. There will not be anyone to have a lunch together with, to complain about clients with, or to brainstorm a new idea, or to go home or a bar with. The absence of those activities can partly cause burnout, in the worst case. In conclusion, being self-employed has disadvantages that can overpower its advantages, and people should never underestimate them. Therefore, it would be wiser if people chose working at companies rather than being self-employed.

question: People should only buy food sourced locally. What are the advantages and disadvantages of this?
essay:
In the modern era, purchasing only locally made food should be preferred by locals. One of the main benefits of this trend is that it can stimulate the country's economic growth; however, It can lead to a decrease in food varieties during different seasons in a year.

To begin with, one prominent benefit of prioritising the locally cooked food is to boost the economy of a country. This is because many people rely on the agriculture sector and small businesses. By purchasing only their products, new jobs can be created, resulting in a stable income. For example, exotic food is hardly seen in Afghanistan because authorities encourage their residents to purchase local made food in order to growing the domestic economy. Thereby, locals should buy domestic ingredients.

On the other hand, the paramount demerit of having only local produce is that it may limit varieties. Certain foods are not produced locally, due to weather conditions in religions, making people deprive of some nutrients vegetables. For example, growing food is an infeasible task in some states of Canada due to the extreme cold conditions in winters. If foods cannot be imported, people will likely experience a scarcity of certain food. From this perspective, locally cooked food has some drawbacks.

To conclude, although purchasing only domestic food can boot the economy of a nation, which is one of the main merits of this trend, it can reduce the availability of some foods, which are essential for survival during different seasons.

question: People sleep less than they used to in the past. What do you think is the reason behind this? What are the effects on them?
essay:
Nowadays, many individuals sleep fewer hours than they did a few years ago. This is mainly because they tend to work longer hours to earn more money, which often results in fatigue.

People tend to sleep less than in the past because they are expected to work more in order to earn higher salaries. The main reason is that many companies encourage employees to work overtime if they want incentives or promotions. Additionally, many private companies exploit workers by not paying them properly. For example, private hospitals often pay only one-fourth of the salary compared to government hospitals. As a result, individuals have to work longer hours, sacrificing their sleep.

The primary effect of not sleeping adequately is chronic fatigue. This affects their day-to-day work and ability to perform with efficiency. Furthermore, this also means that they underperform at work because of being too tired or irritable around those at work or could even have health problems. For instance, a study shows that people who slept for 8 hours worked more activitly and efficiently than those who did not get enough sleep and developed more health issues.

To conclude, many individuals sleep for fewer hours than that of the past because they are expected to work more to earn high salaries, which results in fatigue to the point of exhaustion.

question: Several languages are in danger of extinction because they are spoken by very small numbers of people. Some people say that governments should spend public money on saving these languages, while others believe that would be a waste of money. Discuss both these views and give your opinion.
essay:
The ongoing debate surrounding whether governments need to spend money to protect languages in danger of extinction has given rise to two distinct perspectives. One side argues that saving these languages should be spent the money, while the other side insists that this policy would be a waste of money and offers a more viable solution. This essay will carefully analyse both positions before presenting a well-reasoned personal opinion.

Those who support the government should spend money to protect almost extinct languages argue that it presents a straightforward and effective way to preserve cultural heritage. One primary argument is that we can learn the traditional customs through languages. For instance, archaeologists discovered the Maya Civilization, which had agricultural development, a hierarchy system, and medical knowledge from learning their alphabet and language. Moreover, protecting languages that are in danger of extinction demonstrates that strengthening ethnic identity and social cohesion, making it a practical consideration.

On the contrary, advocates of governments should not waste public money on preserving languages, contend that it offers a more comprehensive and sustainable policy. The rationale key behind this stance is that it should use the public money on enhancing public welfare such as constructing roads, building public infrastructure, and improving other social issues, which prioritizes the majority of people’s rights and ensure that it could take care of those who truly need assist or improve in the society, thus addressing not only current issues but also preventing future and long-term benefits.

In conclusion, both sides of the argument concerning whether governments need to spend money to protect in danger of languages provide valuable insights. However, after careful consideration, I am inclined to believe that spending the money on the other way instead of preserving languages offers balanced and sustainable results, particularly when viewed from a long-term perspective.

question: Some businesses find that their new employees lack basic interpersonal skills such as cooperative skills. What are the causes?
essay:
In businesses, a skillset and knowledge packages owned by new employees will determine the success of doing some tasks. Various skillset, both harskill and softskill, need to be complemented and improved by new employees. For hardskill, the competencies to solve a job with high quality is mandatory. However, softskill which is a skill beyond technical aspect is also crucial, mainly related to interpersonal skill. In this context, to build a strong foundation of those two skill for new employees is challengng and need to be match with the industry needs. For new employees, hardskill can be honed by building the academic skill from formal education. Nevertheles, softskill like interpersonal skill need to be improved by increasing a lot of experiences.        

Lack basic interpersonal skills may be occur dute to several reasons. First. in formal education, new employees only focus on how to accomplish a bundle of tasks and pass the exam. They do not follow and be active on some events or organization, which can build a connection and cooperation with others that related to improve cooperative skills. Second, the businesses culture with a varied generation and different age gaps can be difficult to face, especially for new employees. They still can not fully adapt with the new culture. Third, new employees usually do not have any experiences before joining the businesses so that they need some guidance and need to be accepted by old employees.

To face those challenges. several solutions can be implemented. First, to build a skillset of interpersonal-related, new employees can be more active in organization. They can be involved in many events and push to organize some roles so that they can have a responsibility to handle the situation which need an interpersonal-related condition and build some connection and improve the networking. Second, employers can held a team building or an interpersonal-related class so that new employees can have a basic skill theory. Third, new employees need to know and adapt the culture from businessess, and also build a communication and strong coordintion with old employees with the new culture so their interpersonal skill is also improved. By designing and implementing those solutions, new employees can improve their basic interpersonal skills, which including cooperative skills so that they are better prepared for face the tasks at hand.

question: Some parents give their children everything that their children ask for or allow them to dowhatever they want to do. Is this good for children? What could be consequences for thesechildren when they grow up?

question: some people believe that students should learn practical skills suh as cooking, budgeting, and car maintenance at school. Others think that schools should only focus on academic subjects.
essay:
While  some people argue that students should learn practical skills such as cooking, budgeting, and car maintenance at school. Others believe that schools should only focus on academic subjects. This essay will discuss both perspectives before presenting my own personal view, which supports a balanced approach.

On one hand, allowing schools to focus only on academic subjects may help students advance their career trajectory and gain a broader understanding of the world. question: To what extent is advertising beneficial or harmful to society?
essay: Advertising is often viewed as a double-edged sword due to its mixed impact on society. On the one hand, it enhances brand awareness, expands consumer choice, and stimulates market competition, which can lead to economic growth. On the other hand, modern advertising frequently fuels consumerism and encourages impulse buying by appealing to emotions rather than reason. Furthermore, intrusive advertising can bombard people with messages, resulting in information overload and concerns about privacy invasion. Some companies even cross the line by exploiting insecurities and promoting unrealistic beauty standards, which can distort priorities among young people. Consequently, although advertising can be justified as a tool for economic development, its ethical implications remain highly questionable.

question: In many workplaces, online communication is getting more common than meeting face to face. Do the advantages of this development outweigh the disadvantages?

question: Present a written argument or case to an educated non-specialist audience on the following topic:Some employers reward members of staff for their exceptional contribution to the company by giving them extra money. essay:

We cannot deny the fact that money plays the most important part of cycling the world's economic and, of course, our everyday life. Hence, the most effective and easiest way to get people' hand on work is no where around but an extra sum of incentives or bonus from the company's annual benefits.
 This hit hard to my personal experience. I realised the essence of it when I worked in a telesale company. I was offered a relatively medium-to-low basic salary which could not even yeild me any allowance after living cost reduction. However, I was fortunate enough to have a monthly incentive which would be evaluated solely on my performance each month.  On the contrary, if there were not given any incentives and were offered with something else as a reward, i.g. a free trip to somewhere, a couple of coupon vouchers, or even a car or house, I would still be ungrateful compared with the extra money earned every month. The reason is simply because stable income is considered more reliable than any other tangible or intagible assets, in the perspective of an employee. On top of that, the incentives and bonus can be increased yearly or quaterly based on the company performance.
 In summary, I would like to reiterate that I agree on rewarding the emplyees with incentives and bonuses. Due to the fact that they can be as tool to build a strong montivation among the employees and deliver the company's gratitude toward them in an appropriate way.

question: Some say that because many people are living much longer, the age at which people retire from work should be raised considerably.To what extent do you agree or disagree?
essay:
People nowadays are living much longer.
 With the discovering of new medicine and with the innovation in the medical field, the life expectancy of a person skyrocketed over the last decade.
 Some people might think that the consequence of this phenomenon should be the fact that the age at which people retire from work ought to be raised considerably.
 I don't agree with this idea.
 Rather than involving the life expectancy of a person, we should consider the job itself.
 Is it tiring? Does it require a person to be able to react immediately to a potential problem?
 If the job is tiring I think that it would be better for a person to retire. If you end up being worn out every day because your job is very exhausting the life expectancy should not be the fact that decides if you have or not the right to retire.
 The question ought to be: Is the person physically and mentally able to cope with the work?
 The older you get the slower you become. This is another pivotal factor that should be taken into account when deciding the retirement age of a person.
 The ability to react to dangerous events keeps the working environment under control.
 Let's imagine a carpenter working on the construction of a new building.
 What if a wall suddenly falls? Does an elderly person react faster than a younger worker?
 I am an optimistic person but I would stick to my guns in this scenario betting on the younger person.
 I would like to wrap up this essay by saying that I could have thought of many more reasons that explain why the age at which people retire from work should not be based entirely on the fact that people are living longer.
 Of course, this can not be ignored but basing this decision only on this fact it is seen by me as a poor approach.

question: Some systems require students to specialize in a limited range of subjects from the age of fifteen. Other systems require students to study a wide range of subjects until they leave school. What are the benefits of these two education systems and which is better?
essay:
Nowadays, due to a wide range of education methods and systems, there are a lot of opinion and debates for which being the best orientation for students. Some experts believe that it is good for pupil to only focus on a few subjects during their school life. However, some criticisms argue that the more subject students have, the more practical knowledge they can require. For me, both of such education systems might have different benefits for students as well as I prefer the latter rather than the former.
 First of all, there may have some evidence for people to reckon the upsides of concentration on mainly subjects, Considering the fact that learning too many subjects can be very time-consuming and they may feel confused as well as overwhelmed when they gain a lot of knowledge at the same time. Given a circumstance, students who are studying in Hong Kong must take the DSE every year from the age of fifteen, the Hong Kong examination and Assessment Authority recommends students do not choose more than seven subjects during the exam. Result from having more practices as well as revisions as much as they can. Hence, if students would like to do a test, they could be more suitable to study the primary subjects.
 Conversely, some criticisms considered that it is probably good for students to pick up variety of subjects in view of finding their interests and avoiding spoon feed information. Since many educators think that Hong Kong education system is too exam-oriented, this way could harm students mental and physical health. For instance, parents who are from Hong Kong complain about the education curriculums are too concentrated on spoon feed information rather than giving more life experience to students once in a while. That is why parents sometimes enjoy to choose various of study tours as well as extra curriculums for their children. Thus, some people tend to having more immersing the experience more than studying a few of subjects in their young age.
 In summarize, it is hard to say which opinion would be the best since different types of education methods may have their advantages and disadvantages. On the other hand, personally, it is the key that pupils will never know what they can done until they tried. That is the reason why I prefer studying vary subjects in their young age than being an expert in the major part.

question: The use of social media is replacing face-to-face interaction among many people in society. Do you think the advantages outweigh the disadvantages?
essay:
The modern world of social interaction changed completely over the past decades. Now many of us spent much more time interracting through social media, messengers for private or corporate messaging. We also use video conference and sharing our workflow remotely.
 As for me we have different needs satisfied by communication formed by different systems in our brain. On the one hand we have we communicate with each other aiming to get a useful information. On the other hand communication gives some non-verbal part like a sense of neing involved into one process, belonging to a circle of friends, collegues or family. These two sides of the process of communication are implemented differently that's why each part gets its own advantages and disadvantages thanks to changing integrating new social network technologies.
 If to talk about an information exchange - as for it became much more efficient with using messages, cloud storages and different coworking tools. But it is true only for those people who use these technology well. As far as I can see many advantages in remote work such as possibility not to waste time for commuting, offices and separate equipment, many people notice that their remote collegues disapperar often and avoid their duties. Some people couldn't concentrate while working at home. As for me people who experince such a problems are just addicted to strong stimules such as face-to face interraction and being managed by their boss. At the same time different internet based tools allow you to share much more information much faster and forever. Now you cannot just forget you task because you can always revise it in your work messenger and cloud folder wherever and whenever you are.
 One another need - is to have a social interraction. Of course ssocial network give us some advantage even in this area - now we can keep our relationships with more people across longer distances. While moving to another city or country we do not feel isolation so much anymore. It allows us to mentain or ralationships longer and stronger. At the same time such an option give people a possibility to avoid face-to-face communication with people phisically close to them. As for me it gives only one disadvantage - it is getting harder to load some parts of our brain which are evolutionary oriented to face-to-face interraction.
 I can conclude that social media interraction gave us some new possibilities and cut off some of the old ones. At the same time modern technologies blur boundaries between a real conversation and internet communication. Now we can perfectly see and hear each-other and even support financially. There are also some devises wich allow you to feel even more - like touches or heartrythm of each other. I think that new social media tools and some next directions of these tools will satisfy our face-to-face communication needs more and more over time.

question: As well as making money, business should also have social responsibilities. Do you agree or disagree
essay:
While generating profit is the primary goal for large corporations, many people argue that they should also be held accountable for social responsibilities. This essay will explain why I strongly agree that businesses must be responsible for their impact on society and the environment.

First of all, embracing social responsibilities can be transformed into a great advantage for attracting modern consumers who are interested in eco-friendly products. Nowadays, customers tend to support brands that hold environmental awareness. If a company actively takes responsibility by using recycled packages or green energy, it can effectively win the trust and loyalty of the public. It can not only build a positive image itself but also acquire long-term support from the consumers. Therefore, investing in social efforts is a wise strategy for businesses. Furthermore, the impact of business operations on the environment is far greater than individuals. For instance, large-scale production often leads to significant carbon emissions and water waste. Compared to individual efforts, improvements made by companies will provide a much better progress on environmental protection.

Undeniably, the core objective of any business is to make a profit, as it is the foundation for covering operating costs and employee payments. If social contributions are over-demanded, it may increase burdens and even lead to bankrupt. However, if businesses focus solely on money while ignoring its duties, it will eventually face severe consequences. For example, using low-quality materials or illegally pouring industrial water waste might reduce costs in the short-term, but once these behaviours are revealed, the company will not only face legal problems but also public boycott, even leading to greater financial losses. Therefore, while profit is necessary for survival, social responsibility is the key to a company’s long-term sustainability.

In conclusion, although a business needs to pursue financial development, this does not conflict with its social duties. Conversely, corporations should view social contribution as part of their investments. They can truly achieve sustainable development through protecting the environment and supporting the community.

question: Buying things on the Internet, such as books, air tickets and groceries, is becoming more and more popular. Do the advantages of shopping in this way outweigh the disadvantages? Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.
essay:
Online shopping is attracting considerably more people year by year, especially, for products such as books, tickets or groceries. While taking into account the drawbacks of this development, it is very important to underline how beneficial it is for both customers and consumers operating in that market. 

After the latest developments in the field of e-commerce majority of biased problems  were successfully resolved; however, many people still doubt about privacy and the appropriateness of goods. First of all, issues regarding  privacy became less severe. Thanks to modernisations, new institutions and laws that protect consumers' rights, people are getting used to trusting in online shopping. Meanwhile, users still have a fear of buying a product with unsuitable or undesired characteristics. For instance, despite, i regularly successfully accomplishing several purchases on the Uzum platform, my grandparents prefer physically visiting shops and markets. They claim, traditional method allows them to buy the desired product of their choice, allowing them to bargain with a seller. 

In contrast, despite the numerous advantages for both market agents that this sector of economics brings, it is rapidly gaining  popularity. On the one hand, producers and distributors benefit from online shopping significantly as it increases  the number of natural traffic. That is because, their range of clients is not restricted geographically, they can go across borders, penetrate to new markets. On the other hand, clients also have their own shares. Obviously, nowadays people are overloaded with their work and development, so they have no time to physically visit ticket offices or mini-markets. However, using online shops and websites, for example uzrailways, alibaba, they can order and get deliver to their home. 

In conclusion, the disadvantages of e-commerce cannot be overlooked , and they are not solved completely yet. However, the positive effects of this modernization offers more facilities, for both sides, seller and buyer, that outweigh all its negative effects.

question: compare the pie chart between germany brazil down below.
essay:
The pie charts compare the age distribution of Brazil’s and Germany’s populations in 2000 and provide projections for 2050.

Overall, Brazil shows a steady increase in the proportions of adults and seniors, accompanied by a significant decline in the youth population. In contrast, Germany is expected to undergo a marked aging of its population, with a substantial rise in seniors and a sharp decrease in adults, while the youth percentage remains fairly stable.

Specifically, in Brazil, the percentage of seniors is projected to rise from 5% to 13%, and adults are expected to grow slightly from 65% to 70%. Meanwhile, the youth segment will decrease significantly from 30% to 17%. Conversely, Germany will see seniors increase dramatically from 22% to 40%, adults decrease markedly from 65% to 48%, and youth dip only marginally from 13% to 12%.

In summary, Germany’s population is projected to age considerably more than Brazil’s, with seniors making up a much larger share by 2050. Meanwhile, Brazil will maintain a higher proportion of adults compared to Germany, indicating a comparatively younger workforce.

question: Discuss how technology has impacted education in recent years. what are the benefits and challenges that come with using Technology in the classroom?
essay:

A group of individuals believes in the advantages of high-tech in education. However, others argue the drawbacks of machinery. Therefore, I would like to shed light on both of these viewpoints.

On the positive side, the primary argument the supporters would put forward is the gains of automation in instruction, especially for learners and teachers in developing the quality of teaching. . This is because mechanics has made teaching through online courses and digital textbooks. In addition, they also believe that scholars with disabilities benefit from assistive technologies. The reason is that mechanisation allows for flexible learning Schedules with virtual classrooms - Online tools. On the contrary, those in favour of the latter opinion have their own arguments, the drawbacks of robotics should not be underestimated. To begin with, they claim that educators face increased workload and stress from managing multiple flat forms. This is because assessing students' progress fairly with online tools is challenging due to issues like cheating. Moreover, it can serve as an efficient way to overcome challenges, such as unequal access to telecommunications and potential distractions. For instance, pupils can be surfing the web or listening to music during class, and the teacher cannot control this.

To conclude and offer my position, there are convincing arguments both for and against regarding the merits and challenges of applied science in the educational system.. However, I believe that the positive effects of electronic components for academics outweigh their limitations. I lean towards the first opinion.

question: Due to television and the internet it is easier today for a large number of people to become famous. Is this a positive or negative development?
essay:
Everyone seeks attention from the public through different channels. Nowadays, many people find it easier to become popular through Television, social media and the internet. I strongly believe that this is a negative development as it will create misinterpretation of the information and overdependence among many people.

To begin with, people create their own space on the internet and television channels to present their view point, ideas and information on different areas. For example, several social media influencers can be seen in the internet and other media who promote the use of daily products and services to the public. Moreover, they try to impose their daily lifestyle on the community , and the viewers get influenced by their way of living and luxurious lifestyle. This would create a negative influence on individuals and may lead to depression as they compare themselves with these influencers.

Another point to consider is that depending on celebrities on life advice or anything through web channels and television reduces critical thinking of people and increases overdependence on them for their needs. They would blindly believe the data presented through entertainment platforms and the internet instead of doing a fact check through research. Studies conducted across several parts of the world show that most of the youth have less thinking capabilities compared to the past.

Overall, while the internet and the news platforms offer several benefits to the public , including easy access to info and communication, their downsides , such as spreading fake information, bad influence on the community and excessive dependence, cannot be ignored.

question: Employers sometimes ask people applying for jobs for personal information, such as their hobbies and interests, and whether they are married or single. Some believe that this information may be relevant and useful. While others disagree. Discuss both views and give your opinion.
essay:
Some people believe that it is important for bosses to have some clue about  applicants' personal lives. This essay will discuss both views before presenting my own opinion.

On the one hand, some firms might think it is important to be a good person as much as being enough for educational needs. For example, in many movies, directors need to be married to have a commercial , or this is  evidence of being a loyal person. This advice may show the execs that  they are suitable for the jobs according to their personal lives. And for the job applicants, this approach gives importance to outside life. As a result, they feel more welcomed and have boundaries.

On the other hand, others argue that it is not relevant to have individual data for firms. This may make people feel like they are not enough academically. Having facts like these is not meaningful for administration. For instance, if an individual is enough to get accepted to a company but not getting into just because of their hobbies, it makes them furious and shows an unacceptable excuse.

In conclusion, although some people believe that personal info about position applicants can help authorities understand candidates better, others argue that such details are irrelevant to professional abilities. In my opinion, execs should mainly focus on applicants’ skills and qualifications, while personal knowledge should only be considered when it is clearly related to the business.

question: Establishing good relationships in the workplace is not important, as the primary goal of every person is to focus on work. To what extent do you agree or disagree?
essay:
It is often argued that establishing good relationships in the workplace is not worthy, as the primary goal of every person is to focus on work. From my perspective, it seems convincing that focusing on interection in the workplace is important.

To begin with, I accept that maintaining good relations with all co-workers is often hard and even counter-productive. Firstly, frequent interection can lower morale. Ignoring the importance of outstanding work performance can result in slow promotion and decreased job satisfaction. In other words, employees in a workplace where relationships are overvalued might feel undervalued, leading to increased turnover rates. In Korea, building a close relationship with managers is significant since it affects performance review and career progression, so many people have no choice but to have unnecessary coffee breaks in the office and get together arter work. Also, maintaining good relations with all co-workers is often hard, and they can put their noses into other’s personal matters and give unwanted advice, wasting time in the workplace. However, when employees focus on their specific roles and responsibilities, they can perform more effectively.

On the other hand, I am convinced that people should keep friendly relationship with their co-workers. First of all, when the work environment is efficient and friendly, conflicts and confusions in the workplace decrease significantly. Less conflicts help employees concentrate better on their tasks at hand, and it results in higher productivity. In the healthcare industry, where accurate communication is crucial, strong relationships among staff can lead to better patient care and fewer medical errors. If they do not communicate often, some patients will be in fatal conditions on suffer from unexpected side effects. Moreover, dismissing the importance of good workplace relationships can lead to poor collaboration among employees. To be specific, a lack of cooperation may hinder the exchange of ideas and reduce opportunities for innovation.

In conclusion, it is idea to establish good relationships with co-workers in the workplace for a pleasant working atmosphere and outstanding work performance. Hence, people need to find a healthy balance between being productive at work and forming social bonds.

question: For a less developed country, the disadvantages of tourism are as great as the advantages. What is your opinion?
essay:
Tourism has become an important industry for many countries, especially less developed nations. While tourism can bring several benefits, it can also create certain problems. In my opinion, although tourism has some disadvantages, the advantages are greater if it is managed properly.

One major advantage of tourism is economic growth. When international visitors travel to a country, they spend money on hotels, food, transportation, and entertainment. This helps increase national income and supports local businesses. In addition, tourism creates many job opportunities for local people, such as tour guides, hotel staff, and restaurant workers. As a result, the standard of living can improve.

Another benefit is the development of infrastructure. Governments often invest in better roads, airports, and public services in order to attract more tourists. These improvements not only benefit visitors but also make life easier for local residents.

However, tourism can also have some disadvantages. For example, large numbers of tourists may cause environmental damage, such as pollution, destruction of natural areas, and increased waste. Moreover, tourism can sometimes lead to the loss of local culture if communities change their traditions to satisfy tourists’ expectations.

Despite these drawbacks, I believe the advantages of tourism outweigh the disadvantages. With proper planning and responsible tourism policies, governments can protect the environment and preserve cultural heritage while still benefiting from tourism.

In conclusion, tourism brings both positive and negative effects to less developed countries, but overall it provides valuable economic and social benefits when it is carefully managed.

question: FOR SCHOOL CHILDREN THEIR TEACHERS HAVE MOE INFLUENCE ON THEIR INTELLIGENCE AND SOCIAL DEVELOPMENT THAN THEIR PARENTS. TO WHAT EXTENT DO YOU AGREE OR DISAGREE?
essay:
t is a common opinion that school educators have more influence on the holistic child development than their parents. Personally, I think neither group is always bigger in every case.

Nowadays, due to the high pace of life, especially in big cities, parents tend to spend less time with children than they used to in the past. Meanwhile, school teachers spend huge periods of time with children. In school, teachers influence how children learn new ideas, how they read and think, and how they work with other students in class. They also regulate and control relationships among students and solve interpersonal conflicts. Teachers also set rules and give praise, and this helps a child feel safe and try new tasks, which aids social growth. Furthermore, they accompany students in different internal and external school events such as olympiads, museum trips, and visits to exhibitions.

Despite spending less time with children, parents still have a crucial impact on shaping early thinking and daily habits. They teach kids how to talk, read, and maintain daily hygiene. Moreover, they convey values and moral guidelines. All of these lay the basis for problem solving and attitude towards study. However, the home and school link matters. As a child matures, school becomes more important for social life, but home life still guides big choices.

Despite the modern pace of life, parents still have the primary influence on their kids. They provide a starting platform for future growth. But teachers finish and complement their work, helping to develop holistic and independent human beings from a small sprout.

In conclusion, I think both are important, and the best result comes from good cooperation between home and school.

question: In education and employment, some people work harder than others. Why do some people work harder? Is it always a good thing to work hard?
essay:
Nowadays, the population who study or are employed is working harder than others. This essay  discusses why certain people work hard and might it is good for experience and useful in their lives.

First of all, some employees work harder than others because of the reward. For instance,  people who work spend more time at the office doing more tasks than other members of staff, have more opportunities for a salary increase and promotion. That is, when one’s salary and promotion depend on the capacity which one performs  during the work process. Additionally, the hard work in studying among some students is often an indicator of a goal-oriented person. In other words, when individuals have a certain goal , and they know that they need to put extra effort in order to reach it, they work hard.

However, this situation is not always easy , and the best solution is to save the work-life balance. Working harder than other colleagues or classmates does not always bring the best results in their lives. As an example, the present young generation is stressed about their future, and is forgetting about other important aspects , such as physical or mental health and human relationships. After losing such things, the desired result might not be as pleasant as wished and set. 

In my opinion, although there are many advantages of hard work, I believe that working harder is not beneficial for people’s health and for their harmonious  life.

question: In many countries children are engaged in some kind of paid work. Some people regard this as completely wrong, while others consider it as valuable work experince, important for learning and taking responsability. Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
essay:
The issue of children engaging in paid work is widely debated. While some people believe that any form of child labor is unacceptable, others argue that certain types of work can provide valuable experience and help children develop responsibility. Both perspectives have valid arguments.

On the one hand, many people strongly oppose children participating in paid work. They argue that childhood should be a time for education, personal development, and leisure rather than employment. When children work, especially for long hours or in unsafe environments, it can negatively affect their physical and mental health. In some cases, children may even be forced to leave school in order to earn money for their families. This can limit their future opportunities and trap them in cycles of poverty. For these reasons, critics believe that allowing children to work encourages exploitation and prevents them from enjoying a proper childhood.

On the other hand, supporters believe that light, well-regulated work can be beneficial for children. Having a small job can teach young people practical skills such as time management, communication, and financial responsibility. It can also help them understand the value of money and prepare them for adult life. For example, teenagers who work part-time after school often gain confidence and independence. As long as the work does not interfere with their education or well-being, many people see it as a positive learning experience.

In my opinion, the key factor is the type and conditions of the work. Hazardous or excessive labor should never involve children, as it can harm their health and education. However, part-time or supervised work that is safe and does not affect schooling can help young people gain useful life skills. Therefore, rather than banning all forms of child work, governments should ensure strict regulations that protect children while allowing them to benefit from appropriate work experiences.

In conclusion, although child labor can lead to exploitation and educational disadvantages, carefully regulated part-time work may offer valuable life lessons. A balanced approach that prioritizes children’s safety and education is the most reasonable solution.

question: In many countries people increasingly talk about money (how much they earn or how much they pay for things in their daily conversations). Why? Is this a positive or negative development?
essay:
As we are living into a monetary society, it's unavoidable for people to discuss about money matters in daily conversation about housing mortgage, salaries and other living expenses. The financial anxiety has become a main source of headache that lingers on especially for the young generation who has to bear the increasing burden of a city life.  When our conversation turns to money issues, it doesn's really help up with our status quo, but only further exacerbates social anxiety by unnecessay comparison and higher expectation. We are widely variated in family background, social classes and opportunities. If our daily social discourses are always filled with monetary issues, it is only widening the social gaps between different groups of people and making our society a place less inclusive for underprevilaged people.
 Additionally, money-oriented conversations might as well shape our world perspective into a very pratical, highly self-interested and boring one, which is full of daily trifles and calculation. In book The Little Prince, he says adult will only appreciate a house when you tell them that it is worth a million pounds. It exactly depicts the moderntime conversations that value things more a monetary basis. But the problem we cannot perch on the path of money and many of our lifelong persuit doesn't rely sorely on our income and expensive services and luxaries. We are neither willing nor prepared to see a world where romance can be purchased, friendship ca be transacted and even life itself has a certain value that can be measured in dollars.
 So I would really like to call on more people to rid money discussions our of their daily chats and share more about what's interesting in their seemingly mundane lives, some recent photos of an outing, newly met friends and others. Create meanings of life not through talks about money, but upon something more important to our own existance.

question: In many countries women and men are working full time. It is therefore, logical for women and men to share household works. To what extent do you agree or disagree?
essay:
I strongly agree with this statement. If women and men are both working full time, they should share household works.
 First of all, if women and men are working full time, and they are working in the same amount of time, logically, the amount of time they do household works should be the sam e. However, if one of them work for longer hours, the household works should still be shared but the other one should do more household works.
 Second of all, everyone have responsibility to do household works because everyone lives in the house, makes the house dirty, creates dirty clothes and dishes. If there is only either women or men doing household works, it is not fair at all, because they both work full time.
 But as I mentioned before, the amount of time doing household works can be arranged, it doesn't have to be evenly shared. For instance, if the woman in this family work for longer hours or got an extra call by work, the man should help and do her part of household works. And when the man are working more, the woman should do the same.  In conclusion, when women and men are both doing full time jobs, they should share household works. But it doesn't have to to be split in two halves, it can have alternatives depending on the working hours or special situations. In this way, the house can be more peaceful and have more harmony without arguments about who should do the household works.

question: In many countries, people are choosing to live a healthier lifestyle by eating organic food products. However, organic foods can be more expensive than non-organic options. question: In many countries, the age at which people can retire is being raised. question: In many countries, the amount of waste produced by individuals is increasing rapidly. This has led to serious environmental problems. Discuss the reasons for this issue and suggest what can be done to address this problem. Support your opinion with relevant examples and explanations.
essay:
Can you count how many times you have bought a product just influenced by advertising? Nowadays, although people pay a lot of money on their routine needs, they spend a great amount of their money on buying useless things. Therefore, the main question is what motivates them to spend a fortune on these unnecessary products. question: In many countries, the use of prison sentences as a punishment is seen as an increasingly ineffective method for reducing crime rates.
