question: In many parts of the world, traditional methods of production are being replaced by robotic systems and computerized processes. Do you think this is a positive or negative development for cultural preservation?
essay:
It is often argued that the rise of automation poses a threat to traditional culture. However, I contend that machinery and digital technology serve as vital tools for preserving both tangible and intangible cultural heritage. I personally disagree with the notion that modern technology and tradition are incompatible; in fact, technological advancements have prevented many customs from vanishing entirely.

A primary example of this can be seen in the textile industry, specifically the art of carpet weaving in rural regions of my country. Traditionally, this craft demanded immense physical stamina, requiring women to labor for several hours a day. Consequently, many abandoned the trade due to the sheer exhaustion involved. The implementation of computerized looms and mechanized processes has since alleviated this physical burden. By making the production process more efficient, technology has actually revitalized interest in this craft, ensuring it remains a viable profession for future generations.

Furthermore, technology plays a crucial role in linguistic preservation. Many of the world’s endangered languages have been saved from extinction because they are archived in digital databases or recorded using advanced electronic equipment. These recordings and digital publications ensure that even if a language is no longer spoken widely, its phonetic and grammatical structures are preserved for posterity.

In conclusion, far from being a threat, robotics and automation are essential allies to traditional culture. By modernizing the methods of production and documentation, technology ensures that our heritage is not only protected but also adapted for the modern world.

question: In recent years, many people have adopted a minimalist lifestyle, choosing to own only essential possessions. What are the advantages and disadvantages of this way of living?
essay:
In recent years, many people have chosen to adopt a minimalist lifestyle. They choose to keep only essential possessions and get rid of everything else. In this essay I will discuss the advantages and disadvantages of this way of living.

Firstly, a minimalist lifestyle is great for people who can’t spare time to clean their living space. For example, a minimalistic apartment is much easier to clean than a cluttered one, resulting in more time for rest, social life and more important things. It is a comfortable way of living for people who work very time-consuming and stressful jobs like nurses, doctors, policemen, lawyers and so on. Consequently, coming home to a clean and tidy space provides comfort and relaxation after a hard day.

Secondly, this way of living is very suitable for big families. It can be difficult to maintain a clean space when living with children, but it is necessary. Children should be provided with a clean and safe environment so they can learn, grow and mature. This eventually results in good grades and education, increasing productivity and creativity.

However, this lifestyle can take away self-expression. I think this choice of living is very subtle and bland. Of course, some people prefer a minimalistic environment, and that is also good, but some people, artists, for example, prefer a space that is theirs to experiment with and to express themselves.

To add, some people might find minimalistic lifestyle cold and unwelcoming. They see their home as a way to show others who they are. Also, to be surrounded by their belongings helps them feel safe and comfortable. As a result, they feel uncomfortable and unwelcome in such minimalistic spaces. 

In conclusion, a minimalistic way of living is a great choice for big families or people who work stressful jobs, because it makes it easier to clean and maintain. On the other hand, people who like to collect different things and to express themselves through their surroundings might find it very boring and uncomfortable.

question: In recent years, more people have chosen to shop online rather than visit physical stores. Why is this the case? Do you think this is a positive or negative development?
essay:
In recent years, online shopping has become increasingly popular, replacing traditional forms of shopping in many places. I strongly agree with this trend because it offers greater convenience and efficiency for modern consumers.

Firstly, online shopping is far more convenient than visiting physical stores. This is because people can purchase items at any time without needing to travel or wait in long queues. For example, many office workers prefer to order groceries or clothes online after work instead of spending hours in crowded shopping centres. Furthermore, online platforms usually provide a wider variety of products, allowing customers to compare prices and select the best options easily. Therefore, online shopping saves time and makes daily life much easier for busy individuals.

Another important reason is that online shopping is becoming increasingly reliable despite some disadvantages. Although traditional stores may offer fresher products such as fruits and vegetables, modern delivery systems are improving rapidly. For instance, many companies now provide same-day delivery and use advanced packaging methods to maintain product quality during transportation. In addition, online reviews help customers choose trustworthy sellers and avoid poor-quality goods. As a result, the advantages of online shopping clearly outweigh its drawbacks.

In conclusion, I believe that online shopping is gradually replacing traditional shopping due to its convenience and improving reliability, making it a positive development overall.

question: In recent years, there has been a rise in the use of performance-enhancing drugs in sports. What are the consequences of doping for athletes? What measures should be taken to combat this issue?
essay:
It is true that in recent years the sports industry has experienced a significant rise in the use of performance-enhancing drugs. This essay will discuss the serious consequences of doping for athletes and argue that stricter regulation and preventative measures are required to combat this issue.

The main consequence of doping is the severe damage it can cause to athletes’ health. Although these substances are taken to increase strength, they often push the human body beyond its natural limits. This can result in long-term medical conditions, particularly cardiovascular diseases, as many performance-enhancing drugs increase heart rate and blood pressure. For example, using too many steroids or taking them incorrectly can lead to heart attacks and hormone problems. In addition, artificial hormones such as testosterone may negatively affect brain function and behavior, sometimes leading to aggression or mood disorders. Consequently, while athletes may achieve short-term success, they risk serious and irreversible health problems.

Turning to the question of solutions, several measures can be implemented to address this problem. First and foremost, sports organizations should introduce stricter anti-doping regulations and conduct regular testing. International cooperation such as the Anti-Doping Agency is essential to ensure fairness in competitions. Furthermore, athletes should receive proper medical supervision and education about the long-term risks of these substances. Preventative strategies, including balanced training schedules and adequate recovery periods, can reduce the pressure that drives athletes to use illegal drugs in the first place.

In conclusion, doping has serious physical and psychological consequences for human. Therefore, stronger regulations combined with education and medical checking are necessary to combat this growing problem.

question: In some countries children take up aid jobs during the summer vacation. Some people feel that this amounts to child labour. Others argue that summer jobs help children learn valuable lessons. Discuss both views and give your opinion.
essay:
In the modern era, more and more people think that states that provide aid and job opportunities for kids in the summer will benefit and build a strong society. However, others argue that countries should not be eligible for these jobs for kids. In my opinion, I think the government should engage kids in these jobs with limited responsibilities.

On the one hand, it is believed by some people that the government should not provide aid jobs for children. Although this will make kids work at young ages, this may affect them when they grow up due to not giving them time for play, rest, and family life. Furthermore, another reason is safety and health. Many aid jobs, such as farm work and street selling, can be risky for children. They may face heat, carry heavy things, or face bad people. Therefore, hiring kids can affect them in the long term.

On the other hand, supporters believe summer work can teach kids a lot of valuable lessons that they can not take from schools. In fact, aid jobs will benefit kids and build their character due to seeing real-life challenges and how they can find solution. Moreover, children will learn how to manage and finance the money they earned from summer jobs, and it will also teach them independence. As a result, giving kids the opportunity to work in the summer can provide a strong society in the future.

To conclude, I believe summer jobs are good only when the work is light, safe, and limited in time.

question: In some countries, there has been an increase in the number of parents who educate their children themselves at home instead of sending them to school. Do you think the advantages of this outweigh the disadvantages?
essay:
There has been a tendency towards home education, which is carried out solely by parents instead of sending children to school. It is difficult to imagine that the perks of homeschooling are greater than its drawbacks, because kids lack social interaction and their parents are not well qualified, although such children can have personalised learning and a safe learning environment.

Students can learn lessons at their own pace because of adjustments to teaching style and teaching style according to their abilities when they study at home. This is largely because lessons are taught depending on their strong and weak points, which help parents to teach school curriculums in the child’s learning speed instead of moving at a fixed pace in school. For example, struggling students who fall behind in classrooms can receive extra attention from their parents in their studies. Furthermore, home education protects children from the possibility of bullying, violence, and negative peer pressure in classes, because at home, they are far from such negative social influences, studying in a safe and controlled learning environment. A clear example of this is that teenagers at school may feel peer pressure to smoke, skip classes, and engage in harmful activities, leading to loss of concentration in class. Consequently, for these reasons, such as a one-size-fits-all education system and harmful social behaviors at school, many parents prefer education at home for their children.

On the other hand, homeschooling does not develop children’s communication and teamwork skills, and their parents lack professional teaching skills in school subjects. As a result, such people become isolated from the community and struggle to cooperate with others. However, schools provide opportunities to interact with groupmates and promote teamwork skills that are crucial in teenagers’ future, particularly in career prospects. Consider the example of two employees in the workplace: the first worker studied at school and attended many school events, and the latter one only received home education and was far from kids. Of course, both of them will be the best in their field, but a homeschooled worker cannot work properly in teamwork duties and will not attend any social gatherings. In addition, parents can sometimes make mistakes in explaining the concepts of some STEM subjects, particularly math, physics, and engineering, because of their low awareness of such curricula. Not only does it cause problems for the understanding of students, but it also prevents children from working in job sectors that are specialized in such kind of subjects. Over time, home education brings difficulties to kids in collaborative environments and misinterpretation of complex concepts.

To sum up, even though home education keeps children away from the failure of schools to accommodate individual learning speeds and harmful behaviours, it cannot create social environments and cover the school lessons professionally as teachers.

question: In today's world, many people argue that it is essential to address environmental issues, while others believe that the primary focus should be on economic development. Discuss both views and give your own opinion. Provide relevant examples and evidence to support your response.
essay:
The global warming crisis and its devastating effects made all the societies to set some legislation for preserving environment in order to keep the earth appropriate place to live. However, plenty of developing countries are against financing on environmental problems due the fact that some economic projects should be ceased for saving the environment. In this essay , I want to discuss both sides to make some consequences.

The environmental issues widely affects  daily life of each human being ,so we cannot get away with it easily. The primary needs of mankind for living lies in the surrounding area he lives. For clarifying this, each man needs appropriate oxygen,food and water supply to maintain his safety and this relates directly to the environment he lives. Considering this, the drawbacks of polluted periphery will cost so much as we are now dealing with. The most important problem is the two centigrade degrees increase in worldwide temperature which obligate the governments to implement infrastructures to avoid excess warming. The other issue is the lack of drinking water due to agriculture and deforestation activities. There are lots of problems which worry environmentalist and compel them to provide awareness for the politics over serious dangers of inactivity.

On the other hand, we have politicians and governments who find that with environmental-friendly rules as a ban for imposing thousand of jobs and they think poverty is more dangerous than environmental issues which may lead dangers in the future. For example, Donald Trump, the vice president of USA,denied signing the environmental appointment which causes a steep economic developments in USA with creation of new jobs which were ceased due to global environment appointment.

At the end, I want to agree with environmentalist of solving current problems in a way that dos not develop poverty and hunger in the world. This means they should offer suitable solutions to persuade the governors.

question: It is suggested that primary children should learn how to grow vegetables and keep animals. Do the advantages outweigh the disadvantages?
essay:
Some people believe that young children ought to breed animals and plant vegetables. This essay will argue about some pros and cons of children working as farmers, and also show that disadvantages overtake the benefits of it.
 One the one hand, it is a fact that young children who work looking after animals and plants learn how to respect the environment and develop an elevated sense of responsability. Certainly, activities such as taking care of animals and grow vegetables favour children in many aspects of their lives, like to have a better health, mostly because of doing lots of outdoor activities and eating healthier food than city children.
 On the other hand, if children learn how to work as a farmer, they could try to do it alone, without any supervision, which is surely a hard work, with a lot of difficult and responsabilties. In order words, those tasks could be dangerous to a child and affect negatively its live. For example, it is necessary to use some special tools to deal with vegetables, and those tools could harm easily a child. In addition, looking after animals and plants take too much time and children must spend their time studying and having fun instead of working.
 In conclusion, although the benefits that people can find in teaching young children to deal with animals and grow vegetables, the disadvantanges of children doing those tasks are extremely high, because of some permanent damages which could be caused by dangerous tools. In my opinion, young children should learn how to do it but they must not do those activities even if they were supervised by adults.

question: It's generally believed that success in fields such as art and sport can only be achieved if a person has natural talent. However, it's sometimes claimed that any child can be taught to become a good sports person or artist.Discuss both these views and give your opinion.Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.
essay:
It is common to think that in fields like art or sport success is linked to natural talent, even though it is stated that children can obtain good results also trough learning and pratice.
 In my opinion, I believe that only a combination of natural talent and sacrifice can lead you to obtain success; for this reason, you can learn and improve several skills, but you will not win against a person with this type of gift, unless this person does not pratice enough.
 To provide an example, if we think about Raffaello, one of the most remarkable painters of all times, and his life we may be disappointed; in fact, thanks to his natural talent he started to paint since he was 8.
 However, we have to bear in mind that he constantly made a research in order to discover new techniques and to gain new inspirations.
 If he was not so focused on his art's improvement, he would not be able to paint an entire church.
 If we move to another field as sport is always more evident the existence of a pattern. Indeed, it is crystal clear that professional players need to pratice everyday, regardless their talent.
 However, Ronaldo this year has winned the nominee for the best football player in the world not only for his innate talent but also for the effort put during training in order to reach always an higher goal.
 In conclusion, through commitment you will obtain good results, but to achieve the highest level you must be gifted with a natural talent.

question: Learning English at school is often seen as more important than learning local languages. If these are not taught many are at risk of dying out. In your opinion , is it important for everyone to learn English? Should we try to ensure the survival of local language and so, how?
essay:
The question of whether to priortise English or local languages is not a matter of choosing one over the other, but rather of finding a balance. While English serves as an undeniable tool for global communication and opportunity, local languages are the bedrock of cultural identity and heritage. A truly holistic education should strive to value both. 

The importance of learning English is an increasingly interconnected world, which is difficult to overstate. It has become the global lingua franca, the common language of international business, science, technology, and diplomacy. Proficiency in English opens doors to vast amounts of information, higher education opportunities and better careers aspects on a global scale. For many, it is a practical tool for economic empowerment and internatinal mobility, allowing individuals from different countries to connect and collaborate. 

Ensuring the survival of local languages is a vital responsibility. This requires a conscious, multi-faceted effort. First of all, governments can implement bilingual education policies where children are taught in their local languages alongside a national or global language like English. Second, the most crucial action happens within communities and families. Parents are encouraged to speak their native language with their children to ensure it is passed down through generations. Finally, leveraging technology to create digital content, dictionaries, and media in local languages can give them a vibrant presence in the modern world, proving they are not relics of the past but living languages of the future. 

To conclude, the goal should not be sacrifice one language for the other. By promoting addictive bilingualism, where English is learnt as a valuable addition to one's linguistic repertoire, we can equip future generations to be both globally connected and deeply rooted in their own cultural heritage.

question: Many aspects of the way people dress today are influenced by global fashion trends. How has global fashion become such a strong influence on people’s lives? Do you think this is a positive or negative development? Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.
essay:
In recent years, global fashion trends have had a significant influence on the way people dress. This essay will explain why global fashion has become so powerful and argue that it is a negative development.

There are several reasons why global fashion has such a strong impact on people’s lives. Firstly, the rise of social media and international media allows people to see fashion trends from around the world instantly. For example, when celebrities or influencers wear certain styles, millions of people are exposed to them and often try to copy their appearance. In addition, global brands promote similar styles in many countries, making these trends widely available. As a result, people are easily influenced by global fashion and tend to follow popular styles.

In my opinion, this is a negative development because it reduces individuality. This is because people may feel pressure to follow popular trends instead of expressing their own personal style. For instance, individuals who choose different or unique clothing may be judged negatively by others. Furthermore, global fashion trends can make cultures less diverse, as people begin to dress in similar ways worldwide. As a result, traditional styles and personal creativity may gradually disappear.

In conclusion, global fashion has become highly influential due to media exposure and global brands, and I believe this trend has negative effects on individuality and cultural diversity.

question: Many believe that modern technology has brought people together, but others say that it has driven us apart. Discuss both viewpoints and give your own opinion. Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge and experience.
essay:
In recent years, there has been an increasing debate that technology has brought people closer. Other critics claim that the said asset is responsible for breaking people apart. 

From my perspective, modern technology is an incredible tool that makes sharing people's life experiences simple.

To begin, several individuals state that the technological change the modern societies are experiencing, has had a positive impact on their lifestyle. Students who moved abroad to seek better tertiary-education are extremely thankful for the technology, since they are able to stay in touch with their family, friends and relatives. Similarly, adults, who are living in a foreign country to search for greater professional opportunities, have the same attitude towards technological tools. A notable example is the considerable number of foreign people who video-call  their family members in the motherland. 

On the other hand, some people strongly believe that this innovation has had the opposite effect. A large proportion of technology users are young individuals, who prefer spending time chatting with people they are unlikely to meet in real life, on platforms such as WhatsApp and Instagram. Consequently, people closer to such technology users are driven far. Additionally, having a face-to-face conversation has significantly reduced, over the past decade, while screen time has substantially increased. 

By contrast, as an immigrant's daughter, I am convinced that the benefits of this resource outweigh the drawbacks.

In fact, this unprecedented invention has made it possible to share people's wins and sorrows, despite them living overseas or hundreds of kilometres away. 

Taking everything into account, the discussed tool has the ability to make people come together and participate in their success, despite the physical distance. However, it can also be responsible for leading close people far.

question: Many people assume that the goal of every country should be to produce more materials and goods.To what extent do you agree or disagree that constantly increasing production is an appropriate goal?
essay:
The population of our planet has been growing at a fast pace and thus meeting the demand for goods has become a tough achievement.
 Some people, in order to face this issue, assume that every country should aim at producing more materials and goods.
 I do not concur with this statement.
 Rather than increase the production of goods and materials, a country ought to sensibilize its citizens not to dismiss goods that are still able to function properly.
 Getting rid of goods that still work just because the new one has been released forces every country to produce more and more good, leading to a worsening of our enviroment.
 Another pivotal factor that I would like to underline is the fact that people from developed countries do not have a shortage of goods. Not at all.
 Consumerism is predominant in these areas and thus these countries should reduce their productions.
 Most of the things they use daily are useless and they serve as amusement. I firmly believe a person can live perfectly without having 5 different cars.
 In my opinion, the fact that a country should aim at increasing its production of materials and goods has another leak that I have briefly mentioned before: a higher level of production would lead inevitably to a higher level of pollution that would be consequently released into the natural environment.
 Eath is already facing some very important issues when it comes to problems related to pollutions.
 Lastly, I think that we should focus on how to produce less or, at least, on how to produce the same amount of goods and materials but in a more environment-friendly way.

question: Many people assume that the goal of every country should be to produce more materials and goods.To what extent do you agree or disagree that constantly increasing production is an appropriate goal?
essay:
Some think that the main objective of the countries should be the increment of production of materials and goods. I partially disagree that the constant increment in production is an appropiate goal becuase it has some negative consequences.
 The idea of increasing constantly the production of materials and goods is based on the globalization process where each export and import goods and products, it is part of the globalise commerce. For instance, in the free trade relationships between coutries, they negotiate the interchange of products, nontheless, in many times those trades produce a surplus or contrary a lack of products that increment the inflation or deflation of prices as a consequence of the invisible hand of Adams Smith that represent the offre and demand.
 Addiotionally, the extraction of some natural resources such as gold, coal and crude are affecting drastically our planet. question: Many people believe that individuals who commit serious crimes should not be released from prison. To what extent do you agree?
essay:
The main purpose of a prison should be to rehabilitate individuals that have committed serious crimes, not to punish these individuals by giving them life sentences. This essay will explain why I strongly disagree with the notion that life sentences should be given out to individuals who have committed serious crimes, as life sentences perpetrate criminal behavior and the only way to truly reduce the crime rate is to focus on rehabilitation rather than punishment.

Every human being has the right to experience freedom, even if they have broken major laws in the past. Of course, this is not to say we shouldn't be lenient on major offenders; however, keeping them in prison for life would not help solve the greater problem. When a person is given a life sentence, it essentially dehumanizes them and deems them unfit for society. They assume that they will never experience the comfort or normalcy that a life outside of prison brings, hence why they give into their criminal urges and can never be truly rehabilitated. Many high-security prisons across the world contain many prisoners who are either serving life sentences or are on death row, and many of these prisons have gangs operating behind bars and violence that occurs daily. These prisoners, due to their current situation, think that they have nothing to lose, and participate in the constant cycle of violence that many of these institutions face. 

 Prisoners should be kept in prisons only until they are fully rehabilitated, as it ensures that they are ready to become a member of society once more. Rehabilitation would be substantially more beneficial to prisoners, as it offers them a chance at redemption. Through rehabilitation, prisoners will learn how they can be contributing members of their community and avoid the patterns that initially turned them into a criminal. For instance, many prisons in Norway are designed to be acommodating and beneficial toward its prisoners. They offer educational services, job training, and recreational activities. By following this method of rehabilitation, Norway successfully keeps its crime rate low, and ensures that individuals once imprisoned will not become repeat offenders.

In conclusion, I believe that life sentences are an unjust way of dealing with criminals, and that rehabilitation should be prioritized to create a safe environment for citizens and former prisoners alike. As rehabilitation humanizes prisoners and eliminates the chance of repeat offenders, it is the best way of keeping the peace in many countries.

question: Many people today, especially in the developed world, are choosing to have fewer children, or none at all.Why is this happening, and do you think it is a good trend?Give reasons for your answer, and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.
essay:
Weather to have children or not is one of the most important questions young adults have to ask themselfs nowadays. Overpopulation is one of the many global problems next to climate change, and poverty.
 In my opinion, the need to have children roots back to us being nothing more, but civilised animals. Animals have one goal above all; reproduce. This is why in many cultures, men and women who cannot have chlidren are seen as disabled, or sick. Nowadays, modern values changed our perspective, having children is often seen as a necessity for a happy, and successful life.
 It is important to recognise the problems the developed world faces. There is smoke in the air, plastic pollution everywhere, poverty in third world countries, and many other factors that make Earth a terrible place for many. Above all, the life we give birth to will have to eat, drink, and consume just like everyone else, using more and more of the already limited resources, generating more and more trash.
 Many people argue that the above mentioned points are a pessimistic and unrealistic. Humans have to reproduce, to keep the species from extintion. And truthfully the only way to do that, is to give birth as many kids as possible. This issue lies deep in our society; here in Hungary, a family is rewared almost 30000 USD if they have 3 or more kids. This means that many people have children because they get money for it, and they are also seen socially accepted.
 I believe modern day intelligence goes further than IQ points and money earned. We have to recognise the problems created, overpopulation being one of them. While I do not believe that we should not have kids, I think there should be careful planning involved and self-reflection before giving birth to new life.

question: Now a days people make new friends through social networks and Internet chat groups. some people think this is good other think that face to face interaction is essential discuss both views and give your opinion.
essay:
In this advanced era, many people think that making friends using social media is  better than having physical interaction with other people. In this essay, I will discuss both views and give my opinion  regarding the same.

To begin with, there are many social media platforms where individuals can make online friends , such as Instagram, Snapchat, Facebork and many more. With the help of the internet , people from different places, cultures and languages connect with each other through texting and calling. Many individuals find it more convenient as it saves time for travelling for having face-to-face conversations, as one can sit at home and easily communicate with each other. It is better for those who are introverted and prefer chatting over meetings.

On the other hand, physical interaction is more beneficial as it helps in understanding the body  better. In-person meetings can easily tell us about the behaviour of another person. In addition to it,facial expressions play a great role in understanding other people's thoughts. For instance, the survey includes stronger friendships among individuals who prefer meetings, whether once a month or a week.

To conclude, both ways are convenient, depending on the people. I believe that real meetings are more essential to know the person  better, as online chatting can create more conflicts because one can't really tell whether the other person is real, genuine , or trying to express.

question: Nowadays it's possible for people to buy many products or pay for treatments that help them to look younger. Is this a good thing or a bad thing?
essay:
Recently, people can look younger by buying some products or treatments. I believe that this is a good thing because this trend positively affects our society. This fashion can strengthen not only our confidence but also our mental health.

A young appearance makes  people feel more confident and energetic. In modern life, outlook has become more important than before. Most people focus on how they look and spend more money on the treatments. My girlfriend has told me ''A good outlook can bring her a delighted mood all day''. This emotion could apply to work and share to others. In this way, people enhance their work efficiency. Therefore, I agreed  people  use items or medical techniques to make themselves younger. 

In addition, many studies have revealed that people's physical conditions can directly affect mental health. In many medical examples, patients who suffered from a long-term physical disease, have a higher probability to gain mental disease. Conversely, if we have a good outlook and body, we could have a good mental condition. In this situation, we can go beyond the age limitations that are set by ourselves. Olders can do more challenges or high-tension activities. It shows that a younger face makes us live in a younger way.

In conclusion, I totally agree that people having many products and treatments that help them to look younger is really a good sign. This tendency made people believed thay still young, give they confidence to face challenges and also let every have a healthy mental condition.

question: Nowadays many schools find it profitable to sell unhealthy food to student, do you agree or disagree?
essay:
These days, many schools have started selling fast food as an alternative to traditional ones. While it is logical that the result may be highly profitable, I disagree that it may have debilitative effects on the overall health of students. This essay will discuss the following topic and give reasons to support the claim.

First and foremost, unhealthy food hurts overall well-being. Fast food contains a plethora of carbohydrates and sugar which can contribute to major health diseases such as obesity or heart attack. Moreover, the quality of the ingredients does not always meet the standard of food safety, and in some cases, it is not checked for consumption by experts, which ends up on our plates as many types of fast food that can lead to food poisoning. Therefore, selling these kinds of food can pose a risk to their physical health.

Moreover, the aforementioned trend can undermine the reliability of the relationship between schools and students. This is because school is a place where teenagers study and accumulate knowledge from teachers as well as school staff; moreover, it is also where they are guided to understand the principles of nutritious diets. If they are fed with snacks and high-calorie food, it will not meet their parents' expectations at all, as schools put profit before students' nourishment. For example, parents will restrain their child from eating in school and may doubt the quality and accreditation of that school.

In conclusion, the disadvantages of introducing fast food as a replacement outweigh its advantages given the direct consequences to health problems and the credibility of the regime.

question: obesity is becoming major problem in many countries. what are the causes of it and what measures can be taken to solve this.
essay:
In recent years, we have witnessed that, health concerns due to excessive weight are becoming common globally, which is affecting both children and adults. In this essay, we will discuss why this is the case, and also look at various ways to handle this.

First of all, this is because of a lack of physical activities among youth, and more options for unhealthy food. The global rise in technology has attracted youth to spend more time on mobile phones rather than going out and playing a sport. For example, nowadays, school students prefer to connect and play games online in their leisure instead of following a physical routine , which could help them to stay fit and active. Moreover, junk food is common among children, although, outside food is tasty, but the fact that it is processed with unhealthy ingredients should not be ignored.  

However, there are numerous ways to handle this issue. First and most important is, promoting a healthy lifestyle. Schools and universities should keep a mandatory class for sports, which will ensure that everyone is engaged in a daily physical activity, and playing a sport daily will also help in building muscle, which is necessary for maintaining a proper body weight. In addition, parents and the government should encourage healthy food habits. For instance, restaurant chains offering high-protein meals should be promoted, and parents should teach the benefits of making better lifestyle choices.

To conclude, even though the government can help promote healthy food, I believe that teachers and parents should take greater responsibility for making their kids' lifestyle better.

question: People now have the freedom to work and live anywhere in the world due to the development of communication technology and transportation. Do the advantages of this development outweigh the disadvantages?
essay:
Although there is an opportunity for people to work and live anywhere thanks to communication technology and transportation which have developed, this does not always have advantages. This essay will demonstrate how such improvements can lead to problems, such as reducing personal communication and team creation. However, I believe that the advantages of this trend outweigh its disadvantages.

On the one hand, the opportunity to work remotely provides significant benefits for employees. Modern communication tools allow people to perform their jobs from virtually any location, reducing the need to commute daily to an office. As a result, workers can achieve a better work-life balance and spend more time on their families and personal interests. Firstly, this flexibility allows individuals to choose places with lower cost of living or better quality of life while still maintaining their career. Secondly, this benefit allows companies to recruit talented employees from around the world rather than being limited to a specific location. 

On the other hand, remote work may weaken communication and collaboration among colleagues. When employees rarely meet offline, it may become difficult to build strong professionals relationships and misunderstandings can occur more easily.  In addition, newcomers might struggle to integrate into a team without direct interaction and guidance from experienced coworkers. These factors could negatively affect teamwork and overall productivity and atmosphere in the team.

In conclusion, although working and living anywhere may create communication challenges with teams, the advantages including improved work-life balance and global employment opportunities, clearly outweigh the disadvantages.

question: People should follow the custom and tradition when people start to live in a new country. To what extent do you agree or disagree.
essay:
When populations move to a new country, they must abide by that location's culture and traditions. I believe that this statement is true to a certain extent, however it is also important that the people moving do not lose their original identity. 

Certain people refuse to assimilate in the societies of the countries they immigrate to, however they should do this in order to not disrupt the locals. Almost all countries have unique customs they follow, if someone new doesn't follow these customs they may come off as disrespectful. For example, in the Middle East it is common that women dress more conservatively than they do in the West. It is reasonable for inhabitants of Middle Eastern nations to expect visitors to not dress too provocatively in order to not disrupt them. Secondly, certain behaviors may be accepted in certain places, however that does not mean that they are appropriate where they currently live. A prime occurrence of this is seen in the United States, where all members of a crowd stand during the national anthem. Even if you are not an American citizen, it is a sign of respect to stand during the national anthem, not doing so would only unnecessarily disturb others.

On the other hand, while it is important to do our best to fit in to a new society, we must also not lose the culture we came from in the process. Humans require a strong sense of identity, and without it they struggle. For this reason, we must always remember where we came from. Greek people demonstrate this in a great way in the city of Warsaw, where they organize monthly cultural events to represent their country and keep in touch with other Greeks. Another reason one must always represent their culture, is because it is a great way to find your footing when moving. It is very difficult to start fresh, and people from your home country can be of great help in making this process easier. A common example is the Indian community, who are very tight-knit no matter the place or time and always try to help one another with all situations. People from your culture, who experienced the same difficulties are more likely to help, and can aid someone in more meaningful ways.

In conclusion, I agree that we must respect the traditions of the nations we move to, but I also believe we must not forget the culture we left. We must preserve our own customs as well as we can, while simultaneously respecting the place we live in.

question: Present a written argument or case to an educated non-specialist audience on the following topic:Some employers reward members of staff for their exceptional contribution to the company by giving them extra money. This practice can act as an incentive for some but may also have a negative impact on others.To what extent is this style of management effective?Are there better ways of encouraging employees to work hard?Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.
essay:
There is no doubt that rewarding employees wirh extra money can have positive effects on the quality of work. However, there are many different ways is which employers can implement to increase theit employees satisfaction in the work environment. In this essay, I will discuss why money incetives can be beneficial, and mention different alternatives.

question: Problems with environmental pollution have become so serious that many countries are trying to solve these problems. giving reason from this essay.
essay:
Environmental pollution has become a serious problem in many countries. Air, water, and land pollution are affecting people’s health and the natural environment. Because of these dangers, many governments are trying to find solutions to reduce pollution and protect the planet.

One major cause of environmental pollution is industrial activity. Factories often release harmful gases and chemicals into the air and water. These pollutants can cause serious health problems such as breathing difficulties and diseases. Another important cause is the large number of vehicles on the roads. Cars and motorcycles produce exhaust fumes that pollute the air and contribute to climate change.

To solve these problems, many countries are introducing new policies and laws. For example, governments are setting limits on factory emissions and encouraging industries to use cleaner technology. Some countries are also promoting renewable energy sources such as solar and wind power instead of fossil fuels. In addition, public transportation systems are being improved to reduce the number of private vehicles on the road.

Individuals also have an important role in protecting the environment. People can reduce pollution by recycling, saving energy, and using public transportation or bicycles instead of driving cars.

In conclusion, environmental pollution is a serious global issue that requires action from both governments and individuals. By working together and using sustainable solutions, countries can reduce pollution and create a healthier environment for future generations.

question: rge businesses have big budgets for marketing and promotion and as a result, people gravitate towards buying their products. What problems does this cause? What could be done to encourage people to buy local products?
essay:
Globalization has a significant impact on the business market. The door has been opened for companies from different nations and these companies spend a huge amount to promote their products to attract customers. This essay will elucidate the problems faced by the local vendors due to this and propose some suggestions in subsequent paragraphs.

To commence with the problems, big companies have an adverse impact on the small suppliers. These businesses make their budgets and hire agencies for the advertising of their products, which cannot be afforded by the local businessman. As a result, customers are shifting towards the big brands and the demand for regional goods is decreasing continuously. For example, the Textile Ministry of India has presented a report showing that 70% of the clothing market is captured by brands such as Levi's, Adidas, and Puma. In addition, it is one of the main reason to increase unemployment. This is to say that businessmen, who cannot compete with these enterprises, have to close their businesses and because of this, the unemployment rate is increased steadily.

On the other hand, for the nourishment of domestic products, the government should provide subsidies and loans at a low interest rate to local vendors. To elaborate, this initiative can help them to invest more money in product quality and advertising of their goods and help them to compete with international brands. Furthermore,  traditional events can play a crucial role in connecting people with their roots. As a matter of fact, school seminars, promotion of goods at local events can enhance the sentiments of people to buy domestic products instead of foreign brands. For instance, the Indian government launched the Khadi program to promote khadi clothes throughout India.

In conclusion, it would be reiterated that although large-scale enterprises have monetary resources to capture the market by advertising and promotion, if the government takes the initiative to help small entrepreneurs by providing them with subsidies and loans, then there is no threat to the existence of the local product.

question: Some believe that children who fail in school will have limited futures. Do you agree or disagree?
essay:
Poor academic performance drives parents to spare no expense on tutors and cram schools, fearing that low grades will close doors of opportunity for their children. They believe that without grades and degrees, their children cannot succeed later in life. I disagree with this view; those who fail academically in their youth can—and often do—achieve self-actualization and fulfillment in adulthood. 

School is not the be-all, end-all predictor of success or ability. Rather, it is an institution that measures conformity to rigid standards and testing formats. Many brilliant “neurodiverse” people (those with conditions like ADHD, autism, and dyslexia) struggle academically because such inflexibility is at odds with their unique neurotypes—and yet, they absorb information quickly via self-paced online learning. A person does not need to be a high-achiever in their schooling years to become educated and competent by learning outside of the system. 

In fact, it is not even necessary to be “educated” in the academic sense to excel and find meaning. There are numerous careers that do not require any such credentials. Artists, dancers, woodworkers, and chefs are all masters of their crafts, but these skills are neither measured nor rewarded in school. They live full, joyous lives all the same—because they have discovered the professions that truly matter to them.

One might argue that those who are not so lucky as to find their path in such roles face limitations due to their lack of higher education credentials. This is true: certain professions like medicine and law are gatekept behind advanced degrees. However, there is no rule that individuals who become interested in such careers later in life cannot resume unfinished formal studies and enter their desired fields. They may even have more successful, happy careers because they pursued what they truly wanted in maturity instead of allowing parents and external influences to steer their courses in their youths. 

Regardless of what self-actualization looks like for an individual, there are many paths to that destination. Formal schooling is only one such path, and perhaps not even the best one for the journey they embark o

question: Some children spend hours every day on their smartphones. Why is this the case? Do you think this is a positive or a negative development?
essay:
There is no denying the fact that using mobile devices is a crucial way to communicate with other people. It is a commonly held belief that more and more children spend most of their time on smartphones. This essay will discuss why this is happening and provide a clear view of whether this trend is positive or negative development.

To begin with, there are many reasons why children spend a considerable amount of time on their phones. One of the major reasons is that there are many members of society who are under eighteen years of age who want to use social media. In other words, using social media platforms, such as Snapchat and Instagram, would allow easier communication with their friends. In addition, a huge number of children use their phones as a source of entertainment. For example, recent studies have shown that a large percentage of teenagers spend a substantial amount of time playing video games, such as Call of Duty and PUBG.

In terms of the negative outcomes which are greater than the positives, children's communication skills in real life are declining over time. It is also possible to say that when a young individual communicates frequently in the virtual world rather than face-to-face interaction, his social skills, like talking to his parents, will decrease over time. Moreover, identity theft is one of the common ways for stealing personal information through the internet. For instance, some countries like Australia have been setting restrictions on using smartphones, especially for individuals who are under the age of 18, which is to prevent identity theft.

In conclusion, there are many reasons why children should not be allowed to use smartphones, which contribute to a substantial decrease in their mental well-being.

question: Some children spend hours every day on their smartphones. Why is this the case? Is this a positive or negative devlopment?
essay:
Smartphones have become our partners in daily life, and we carry them everywhere we go. When going to the office, we keep them in our pockets, and after arriving, we open them to contact our loved ones. On the way home, we may use our phones to listen to music or read e-books, and before going to sleep, we often check them one last time even when there are no notifications. Because of this habit, many people have become dependent on smartphones. This raises an important question about the newest generation, known as Generation Alpha or often nicknamed the "iPad Kids." This essay will discuss my experiences with social media and children in kindergarten and elementary school, including the positive and negative effects of smartphones and what the older generation can do to guide them.

One benefit of smartphones is that they make life easier and improve communication with others. However, this convenience can also create problems if not used wisely. For example, a young mother may give her phone to her child because he cries while she cooks. This is not always wrong, but if she repeats this habit too often, the child may become dependent on the smartphone. With little supervision, this dependence can lead to negative outcomes. This example shows that many problems begin with simple situations in the family. Instead of always giving a phone, parents can calm children by talking to them, distracting them with stories, or playing together using toys such as puzzles or building blocks. Therefore, parents should stay calm and guide children patiently without relying too much on smartphones.

However, the situation becomes more challenging when children are already addicted to smartphones. In Indonesia, data shows that about 37.02% of children aged one to four and 58.25% of children aged five to six frequently use smartphones for long periods. These numbers make us wonder what causes this addiction. To understand children better, parents should ask questions instead of immediately forcing them to stop. For example, parents can ask what children enjoy most when using their phones. Children may use smartphones because they feel bored, lonely, or want attention. After understanding the reasons, parents can replace screen time with healthier activities such as sports, drawing, reading, or social interaction. Parents should also explain both the negative and positive effects of smartphones. Negative effects may include emotional problems, learning difficulties, and physical symptoms such as eye strain. Positive effects include access to information and opportunities for learning. As a result, the older generation should guide children toward responsible smartphone use.

Smartphones have both positive and negative effects depending on how they are used. They play an important role in the lives of the younger generation. Both children and adults should learn to use smartphones wisely. By setting a good example, the older generation can guide children toward healthier and more balanced lives.

question: Some parents want their children to read only serious educational books at all times. They don’t want their children to read entertainment books because they think it is a waste of time. Do you agree or disagree with this opinion? Why?

Therefore, they will likely be reluctant to read books. What is more, even they have the right to select books they like, meaning that reading books should be an enjoyable hobby, not a pain. 

 In conclusion, as studied above, I personally believe that some parents should not allow their kids to read books for certain purposes since books can improve their imagination, expand their vocabularies and they have the right to read what they want.

question: Some people believe that future jobs will depend more on creativity than on hard skills. Do you agree or disagree?
essay:
In modern society, job requirements are changing because of technology and global development. Many people believe that creativity is becoming the most important skill for future employment. However, education and technical knowledge still play a central role in professional success. This essay argues that future jobs will continue to depend more on hard skills than on creativity because technical knowledge remains essential and creativity often develops from training and experience.

The first reason is that hard skills are necessary in most professional fields. Jobs in areas such as engineering, science and technology require specific technical knowledge in order to function effectively. Without strong training and education, employees would struggle to complete important tasks. Research suggests that workers’ education level creates more productivity than creativity alone (Gattegno, 2017). This shows that structured learning and professional training are key factors in workplace performance. In addition, Gattegno (2017) explains that "creativity is only a starting point." This statement clearly shows that creativity by itself is not enough to guarantee success. Practice, discipline and technical ability are required to turn ideas into real results. Therefore, future jobs will continue to rely heavily on measurable skills and professional knowledge.

The second reason is that creativity often depends on hard skills. Many creative ideas are developed through scientific understanding and technical experience, especially in STEM subjects such as science, technology, engineering and mathematics. Employers still expect graduates to have strong academic backgrounds and technical knowledge (Brett, 2018). Furthermore, graduates need a mix of soft skills and technical skills to succeed in the workplace (Brett, 2018). This means that creativity works together with hard skills rather than replacing them. Even though creativity can improve innovation and problem-solving, it cannot replace the importance of technical competence. As a result, future employment will continue to depend more strongly on hard skills.

In conclusion, creativity is valuable in modern workplaces, but it is not more important than hard skills. Technical knowledge and formal education remain the foundation of professional success. Creativity can support performance, yet it cannot function effectively without training and experience. For these reasons, future jobs will depend more on hard skills than on creativity.

question: some people believe that loss of animals and plants as major environmental issue while others think that there are other major environmental problems. what is your opinion?
essay:
In the contemporary era, environmental degradation has become a concerning issue in some parts of the globe. There is considerable debate regarding whether either the extinction of flora and fauna or other environmental problems ,such as pollution, is the most pressing issue.

To begin with, some proponents of the view argue that the depletion of vegetation and wildlife can lead to a loss in our ecosystem and soil degradation. Many crops are heavily dependent on bees and other pollinators; similarly, tress act like an underground net, which holds soil for crop yields. Hence, global food security is the risk without them. For example, honey bees are important to boost cotton lint weight, seed set, and fiber. Without them, the crop is not feasible. In this perspective, plants and animals should be protected.

On the one hand, I contend that some environmental predicaments, such as pollution, contribute to climate change, global warming, and the deterioration of human health. This is because not only can vehicles, factories, and multinational organizations emit obnoxious gases in the atmosphere, but they can also release contaminated water in the ocean, lakes, and rivers. This leads to a rise in the atmospheric pollution and water contamination, endangering the entire ecosystem. To illustrate, in India, people who live near Ganga River experience health-related problems, such as skin cancer, and other diseases due to drinking and bathing in the polluted water. 

To conclude, even though a decline in plants and animals can affect our ecosystem and soil, I believe that other environmental impediments, such as pollution, are more crucial to prevent the existence on the earth. Ultimately, if we solve the water and air pollution, we can save the lives of flora and fauna.

question: Some people believe that online learning is more effective than traditional classroom learning. Discuss both views and give your opinion.
essay:
In today’s globalized world, the issue of ONLINE EDUCATION VS TRADITIONAL CLASSROOM LEARNING

has become increasingly prevalent. It is a subject of heated debate, with some individuals emphasizing the numerous benefits associated with ONLINE EDUCATION, while others highlight its potential drawbacks. This essay will explore both perspectives, weighing the merits and disadvantages, before presenting my own viewpoint.

To begin with, assessing whether [ONLINE EDUCATION] is advantageous or detrimental requires careful analysis of several factors. Many proponents argue that ONLINE EDUCATION plays an indispensable role in contemporary society. Its influence can be observed in various spheres, including accessibility, flexibility, and cost‑effectiveness. For instance, the positive effects of [ONLINE EDUCATION] are evident in how it fosters access to education for students who cannot attend physical schools due to distance or financial limitations. A report by The Daily Star revealed that over 80% of individuals experienced notable improvements in their academic progress through [ONLINE COURSES]. This underscores the widespread belief that ONLINE EDUCATION is crucial for the advancement of modern education.

However, opponents contend that ONLINE EDUCATION also comes with its own set of challenges. They argue that it is responsible for several negative consequences that affect not only society at large but also individual well‑being. One major concern is that [ONLINE LEARNING] can reduce face‑to‑face interaction, leading to weaker communication skills and feelings of isolation. Additionally, students without reliable internet access may fall further behind, increasing educational inequality. Therefore, it is essential to carefully evaluate these drawbacks before fully embracing its advantages.

In conclusion, ONLINE EDUCATION has both significant advantages and notable disadvantages. While the negative aspects cannot be ignored, I firmly believe that the benefits of [ONLINE EDUCATION] in today’s world are substantial and essential for progress. Nevertheless, a balanced approach is crucial to ensure that the positive effects are maximized while minimizing potential harm.

question: Some people say that advertising is extremely successful at persuading us to buy things. Other people think that advertising is so common that we no longer pay attention to it. Discuss both these views and give your own opinion
essay:
Some people think that advertising is keep luring consumers have irrational consumption, while others believe that advertising has been integrated into our daily life and no longer drive our attention anymore. In my perspective, there is no doubt that advertising still influence our consumption habits and product reference subtly.

The all-round spread of advertising is the main reason that can shape consumer's purchasing decision. In the all-media era, ads are everywhere: product placement in movies, recommendation haul videos on social media, and outdoor billboard on streets, covering all age groups online and offline. Branding also hire celebrities for endorsements and create anxiety, such as body shape or children's education through ads. As a result,consumers are tend to choose familiar advertised brands for a sense of reliability; fan buy idol-endorsed products, and anxious people purchase unnecessary goods. For example, youngsters often pick skincare brands recommended by influencers, and parents sign up for extra tutoring classes guided by ads. This forms a consumption inertia and leads to irrational buying behaviors.

However, excessive and improper advertising can stimulate consumers' rebellious psychology and lower their purchasing willingness. Some merchants play long in-stream ads before videos or overstate product functions in false advertising, like health products claiming unrealistic benefits, This cause strong resentment, making consumers avoid such brands and even damage their reputation. A case in point is an internet-famous drink, whose sales plummeted after being complained for false fat-reducing claims in ads. Of course, this resistance only targets bad ads, and high-quality ones till effectively influence consumers' choices

To sum up, advertising obviously stimulate consumers to spend. If ads were ineffective, enterprises would not invest heavy in creative and omni-channel advertising. Though improper ads cause occasional resistance, this is just an individual phenomenon. Nowadays, ads are more diverse and hidden, influencing people's consumption choices unconsciously, and their guiding role remaining significant.

question: Some people think it is important to protect traditional culture. Others believe modern culture is more important.Discuss both views and give your opinion
essay:
There is considerable debate over whether it is more important to protect traditional culture or to focus on developing modern culture. While some people argue that traditional culture should be preserved, others believe that modern culture plays a more significant role in today’s society.

Some people believe that, protecting traditional culture is essential because it strengthens national identity and social cohesion. Traditional culture embodies the history, values, and beliefs passed down through generations. By preserving these cultural traditions, people gain a deeper understanding of their roots and develop a stronger sense of belonging and national pride. Additionally, culture plays a vital role in economic development. In many regions, traditional culture is closely linked to local economic activities, such as handicrafts, festivals, and folk performances. Preserving traditional culture not only provides employment opportunities for local residents but also attracts tourists to experience and learn, supporting the growth of the cultural tourism industry.

Others ,the other hand , argue Modern culture can promote communication and understanding. In today’s globalized world, the interaction of diverse cultures broadens horizons and fosters mutual respect, helping people of different backgrounds live together peacefully.  Furthermore, modern culture can inspire cultural innovation. This is because a country can view its local culture objectively, absorb nutrients from other cultures, and make its native culture more vibrant and inclusive. In this way, local culture can expand its influence around the world..

In conclusion, although preserving traditional culture is valuable for maintaining national identity and supporting tourism, I believe modern culture is more important because it fosters international understanding and inspires innovation in an increasingly interconnected world.

question: Some people think more public money should be spent on roads and motorways than the public transport system. To what extent do you agree or disagree?
essay:
It is often argued that government funds should be invested in roads and motorways rather than public transport. I am convinced that spending public funds on public transport has considerable implications for both the environment and road users. 

A key advantage of investing in the public transport system is that it helps eliminate traffic congestion. This stems from the fact that well-maintained and user-friendly roads stimulate the transport movement fast and free. Should the  government replace rough roads with flat, safe roads, without excessive restrictions, even if they have paid entry, users are more likely to choose them over stressful roads. Plus, the government encourages the maintenance and improvement of the road system using taxes and funds collected from road users. For instance, people are very happy to have a vehicle-friendly road which requires the amount of money for taxes in the USA, especially  during the rush-hour and you're running late for somewhere. Thus, paying attention to roads encourages road users and helps prevent traffic congestion. 

A further important benefit of improving the public transport system through government funding is to prevent detrimental effects on the environment. By reducing excessive private vehicles, public transport contributes to a positive effect on blooming environmental areas. This not only alleviates traffic congestion but also contributes to a more sustainable urban environment. For example, in South Korea , applying a new role once a month, which is called “ a day without using cars” to prevent nature from carbon emissions through vehicles. In the long run, the government should invest traffic system to enhance both nature and people's lives.

In conclusion, having considered both perspectives, I firmly believe that investing in public transport is far more beneficial than spending on motorways.

question: Some people think that it is better to educate boys and girls in separate schools. Others, however, believe that boys and girls benefit more from attending mixed schools. Discuss both these views and give your own opinion.
essay:
Some individuals believe that it is better to educate both genders in same school while others argue that boys and girls should attend separate schools. In my opinion, although it offers many benefits like a sense of equality, many religious boundaries may not allow girls to attend mixed schools,

To begin with, educating girls and boys in mixed schools has many advantages. Firstly, it creates a sense of equality among children. This is because every student in a classroom is treated with same respect and facilities irrespective of their gender. Thus, it teaches students unity and sameness. Moreover, students learn to get comfortable around each other. For instance, many young girls may feel shy to talk to boys in their classroom. By taking part in group projects together, they learn to communicate effectively with the opposite gender.

On the other hand, educating in mixed educational establishments can create some issues. First and foremost, many teenagers may get involved in romantic relationships at a young age. This may distract them from their studies and can affect their academic performance. Moreover, heartbreaks from such relationships may cause depression and anxiety among young students. Secondly, many girls with religious boundaries may not be allowed to attend co-education. For example, many women in Pakistan can not complete their education due to family restrictions.

To sum it up, although co-education has many benefits such as promoting unity, it also has many significant drawbacks, which can not be ignored. Therefore, in my opinon, educating girls and boys in separate schools is better.

question: Some people think that modern communication technology is having a negative effect on social relationships. To what extent do you agree or disagree?
essay:
Some people think that modern communication technology is having a negative effect on social relationships. To what extent do you agree or disagree?

Over recent years, more and more individuals consider that the way of technological communication is having an unfavourable impact on social relationships. From my point of view, I totally agree with the given idea for a number of plausible reasons.

One argument is that it reduces direct interaction between people. The population mostly rely on keeping in touch via chatting, video calls or social media platforms, which makes people less communicative in real life and less able to understand others from a personal view. For instance, chatting with your friend may affect your connection that leads to a lost friendship. As a result, people gradually become less comfortable with face-to-face conversations and their social skills may weaken over time.

Another argument why technological communication can negatively affect social relationships is that it allows people to ignore messages and can create confusion. In online communication, individuals may read a message but choose not to reply, which can make the other person feel unimportant or disappointed. Moreover, text messages often lack facial expressions and tone of voice, so the meaning of the message may not be understandable. For example, a short reply might seem bad-mannered or impolite even if that was not the meaning. As a result, ignoring messages and wrongly understanding texts can weaken relationships and create distance between people.

In conclusion, technological communication has significantly changed the way people interact with each other. Although it makes communication faster and more convenient, it can also weaken real-life relationships. Therefore, people should try to balance online communication with face-to-face interaction to maintain strong social connections.

question: Some people think that outdoor activities are more beneficial for childrenâ€™s development than playing computer games. To what extent do you agree or disagree?
essay:

 To commence with the first reason, outdoor activities are good for children's health. As children run and play with ones' friends, outdoor activities will apparently relieve the stress. Furthermore, it is scientifically proven that an exercise is beneficial for all mankind as it is recommended by the doctors and scientists. For instance, doctors encourage the patients who have non-communicable diseases, known as lifestyle illnesses, to exercise more. Thus, it will prevent children from getting illnesses.
 Additionally, spending time with one's friends will definitely help a child to build a healthy relationship with others. As a child learns universal values and realizes important aspects of life, it will greatly influence the way a child will live one's life. For example, a child can learn sportsmanship while playing sports with his or her friend.
 On the other hand, playing computer games can lead a child to be addicted with electronic gadgets. Addiction is one of the most risky things which may put one's life into a pit. If a computer game consists of violent or aggressive contents within it, a child may get influenced. There are a great number of reports and thesis which focus on how computer games affect the children's behaviour in a negative way.
 Moreover, electrical gadgets affect one's eyesight. Nowdays, as most of people are exposed to the lights from the devices, it can be easily proven that there are more people with glasses comparing to the past. Using a computer for a short period of time is not risky, however, using a computer for a long time for certain purposes, even if it is for research work, it will affect one's eyesight badly.
 To conclude, it is proven that outdoor activities are more beneficial than playing computer games. Therefore, communities, parents, and teachers ought to encourage children to do outdoor activities rather than ordering a child be sitted for a long time, the act which causes unhealthiness.

question: Some people think that social media has replaced face-to-face interaction between people. Do you think this is a positive or negative development?
essay:

    On the one hand, in terms of disadvantages, according to the given data, there are many dramatic points in direct face-to-face interaction. One important advantage is the emotional connection between people. Direct interaction allows old people to understand their body  more clearly. For instance, meeting someone directly can make relationships perfect and create stronger love. Furthermore, face-to-face action often reduces misunderstanding that may occur in online messages. As a result, direct communication remains an important part of human relationships.

     On the other hand, in terms of advantages, according to the given research, there are many significant points in the use of social media in online interaction. This is due to many positive marks. One important advantage is speed. People can send messages directly without travelling long distances. For example, friends and family can communicate easily by applications such as WhatsApp or video calls. In addition, social media reduces the cost of communication. Many online platforms allow people to talk for free. As a result, communication becomes easier.

In conclusion, in terms of advantages and disadvantages, according to the new reports, there are many benefits. However, there are many hard parts. Thus, the government recommends people follow the right guidance for a positive result, a better life and a new future.
