question: Write about the following topic.Some organisations believe that their employees should dress smartly. Others value quality of work above appearance.Discuss both these views and give your own opinion.Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.
essay:
It is commonly believed, that companies should have dress code policy for the employees, whereas some people consider that the quality of employees' work is more important, than the appearance of the employees.
 I think there might be some advantages to have a dress code, but disadvantages overweight benefits in this case.
 First of all, dress code policy mandatory for some organisations' workers as it's part of the safety policy. For instance, workers of some scientific or technical labs should wear some specific dresses as their job is with the dangerous chemical elements. The absence of the protecting dress or some details, such as glasses or gloves, might be harmful for the employees. In this case, I believe to have harsh rules for the dress code, is essential.
 Turning to the drawbacks of the dress code, I would mention that it might be uncomfortable and as a result effect on the employees' work quality. For instance, some banks require female employees to wear heels' shoes or male employees to have a tie. This kind of dresses or dress' elements might be not comfortable for the employees and as a result to have a psychological negative impact, such as lack of attention or willingnes to work properly . This will lead to the decrease of the work quality.
 Another drawback of the dress code, is standartisation, when all employees should wear the company uniform with company logo. In this case, employees do not have opportunity to show their unique appearance and identity as all employees have approximately the same appearance. This might have again it's negative impact on workers' perfomance.
 In conclusion, there might be some benefits to have a dress code, but most of all I see drawbacks of this policy, as it might effect on the employees' work quality negatively. I recommend that, companies should consider employees opinion as well before puting into practice dress code policy.

question: Write about the following topic.Some people believe that school children should not be given homework by their teachers, whereas others argue that homework plays an important role in the education of children.Discuss both of these views and give your own opinion.Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.
essay:
Nowadays people's opinion regarding homework for school children is that it should not be given, on the other hand some people are not agreeing. In fact, they believe that homework is a crucial part for the pupil education.
 Personally speaking, I am convinced that homework are positive tools for scholar to remember and pratice what they have learnt in class, but it should be a small ratio rather than what they are currently asking.
 From my personal experience, I learnt that at the school age, children do not comprehend the importance of aknolwedge information in each different courses. As a result of it, pupil only focus on what they like, discarting subjects that are not in their taste and this is when homework plays a fundamental part. It helps scholar to fix in their memories the lesson they attended and from there building more ideas about it. Consequently, following the next courses will be easier for them. Not only does it help them to learn what has been thought, but also it gives them a method of study and habits which will be crucial for university or personal aknwoledgement. It set up the base to thrive on their own in the future.
 Having said that, it is understandable if some people are against homework. Teachers might be given a heavy homework load to students, which is unfair because the children should also have the time to enjoy their childhood, playing and having fun. On the other hand, saying no to homework in their full is incorrect. If scholar are not having any homework, they will lose opportunity to learn and in the long run it will affect their productivities.
 To conclude, I strongly believe that the benefits of homework outweigh the drawbacks. Therefore, homework should be given in order to allow student to understand how to flourish indipendently, but simultaneously, the amount of homework load should be re-evaluated.

question: Write about the following topic.Some people believe that teaching children at home is best for a child's development while others think that it is important for children to go to school.Discuss the advantages of both methods and give your own opinion.Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.
essay:
For some people home schooling is considered to be the right decision for kid's development, meanwhile other people believe that is important to send children to school. I think that sending children to school give them a great advantage not only in their academics but also in their development and social skills.
 Having children at home and be their teacher is not easy. Although, it may be important for their development. A good side of home schooling, is the chance to be more connected with your own family and being able to manage your own time without having strict schedule. This means that you don't only have to study the subjects that are for example taught in school, but also it gives the children a chance to invest their time in activities in which they are interested in.
 On the other hand, sending kids to school gives them the chance to interact more with other people and develop social skills. They have more opportunities to create connections and interactions with other students with the same age and also it might be considered more helpful to be in the same situation with others, so that they can learn to help each other.
 However, in my personal experiece being in a class with a lot of students can be hard. Find the "right people" to be with might be challenging but at the end it is satisfying, because you might also end up having friends for life and creating good memories,
 In conclusion, these two different methods are both acceptable, as long as the child best interest is done, no matter if his being teached at school or at home.

question: Write about the following topic.Some people prefer to spend their lives doing the same things and avoiding change. Others, however, think that change is always a good thing.Discuss both these views and give your own opinion.Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.
essay:
Change is considered a positive thing by some people, whilst other would rather have the same routine avoinding any different activities. Hence, it is complicated to agree or disagree with one of these way of thinking, the choice is personal. However, trying something outside of the normal routine could bring improvement in lifes.
 Change is an important topic in our modern world where everything moves and evolves so quickly and people do not have the time to reflect on the present. Therefore, it is understanding if someone is afraid of it and prefer to have the same habits, spending their life doing the exact same circle of thing week after week. This is call comfort-zone. Everyone aims at it at some point of their life because it is reassuring, easy and releaxing. But does it brings in people's life important events or news? The answer most likely is no. If people do not go out of their comfort-zone, choosing changes over habits, they will never experience something new. In the long run people become unsatisfied, depressed holding negative thoughts and unhappy.
 On the other side, changes are bringing new scenario in mankind's life that can improve, most of the time, their situation. For instance, when a human-being is frustrated by his/hers job, he/she should try to get a new one. Best case scenario he/she will feel more appriciated by it. The point is that this person has not lost anything by this change, because he/she still have an employment. I strongly believe that changes is a good thing because the way people feel during the process of changing is exciting.
 to sum up, Every mankind should live without regret, the change might not bring an effectively positive metamorphosis, but there is still somenthing to lear out of it.

question: Write about the following topic.Some people think living in big cities is bad for peopleâ€™s health.To what extent do you agree or disagree?Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.
essay:
There has been a lively discussion revolving around the megapolice influence on human health, as some people presume living in large cities results in a variety of problems. In my humble opinion, I consider big-city life to be harmful.
 A row of arguments can prove this point of view. The air and water pollution in cities tend to provoke a spectrum of lung and skin diseases. A number of people encountering lung cancer, chronic bronchitis, eczema and psoriasis prevails in megapolicies compared to small towns and villages. Even I have acquired chronic bronchitis while leaving in Moscow. It also must be mentioned that constant contamination of enviroment has long-term consequence for human genetics. For instance, several decades ago the phenomena of allergy was not so widespread. And nowadays, several hundred thousands of people are suffering from it, as their immune system responds inadequately to ordinary things, such as animal fur or dust.
 On the contrary, the medicine level in small villages cannot be compared with medicine in large cities. Some urgent problems such as limb break, infection, serious inflammation, heart attack, seizures or appendicits can be tackled only in large cities, where you can call an ambulance and it will come in a blink of an eye, while in countyside it can cause a dramatical outcome. My friend's grandpa in a distant village broke a rib and it stabbed his lung, and, unfortunately, ambulance did not make it in time.
 To sum up, living in megapolice can possibly extend your life, but for me the quality of this life is still doubtful.

question: Write about the following topic.Some people think that art is an essential subject for children at school while others think it is a waste of time.Discuss both views and give your opinionGive reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.
essay:
It is believed by some that art is a fundamental subject in school, whilst it is considered by others as a waste of time. In my opinion, since only art permits the develop of children's creativity, it has to be considered as important as the other school subjects such as science or history.
 In 2018, it was conducted a research among 100 children in which only half of them were following art classes. The resulting outcome was mind-blowing: in fact, those which had the opportunity to learn more about art had develop their creative skills, while the others, athough some of them had a natural talent, had not made any improvement. Hence, the result has underlined the importance of this subject.
 On the other hand, some people think that children use the hours of art in school only to have fun and play with colours. I believe that this is partly true; however, since children have the right to express theirselves, this time in which art is taught plays an important role in their education. For instance, many studies have underlined that children suffering from ADHD (a psychological issue that causes hyperactivity), since they are using their energies painting and creating sculptures, can be calmer thanks to art.
 In conclusion, I believe that art has to be taken into consideration with the same importance given to the other subjects because it leads to several benefits for children and, for this reason, teachers have the duty to teach them art properly, thus permitting children to become artists one day.

question: Write about the following topic.Some people think that government is responsible for the rise in obesity in children.Do you agree or disagree?Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.
essay:
Obesity among children has risen in the past years. Parents and government share the responsibility to keep children well-being. This essay will discuss reasons and examples of how both sides have responsibilities toward the children.
 To begin with, Politicians should have an obligation to legislate laws that stop some food companies from selling high-calorie diets in the country. Laws toward diet companies should ban them from increasing sweeteners in candies to rise profits. In the same manner, the government has a duty to build outdoor play-ground and clubs to help parents raise healthy kids. Gardens should be grown to overcome obesity. In the UAE each block in the city has its own garden and playground. Research in the Netherlands shows a huge decline in obese children after the government renewed old playgrounds.
 Parents on the other hand share a tremendously important rule. Parents should encourage their children to practice sports and substitute bad habits. Pampering the kids with a sweetened diet should be stopped. Parents should read about the effect of obesity on their kids' health. For example, simple sugar rise the glucose level rapidly causing the unexpected energetic status of the kid before bedtime. Some medical cases were seen among children related to obesity like cardio-pulmonary illnesses. If the parents were aware of these consequences they would discourage the bad habits of their children.
 To sum up, government do its role in keeping the kids' wellness by building institutions and providing laws on their behalf. Also, parents should educate themselves and their children, and stop unnecessary food habits.

question: Write about the following topic.Some people think that government is responsible for the rise in obesity in children.Do you agree or disagree?Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.
essay:
In today's society, obesity in youngster has become a crucial matter for the health system after noticing the skyrocketing rates. It is argued that the government should be held solely responsable for this pandemic, but I personally disagree with this statement.
 The government is already taking measures to prevent this devastating issue. Firstly, it is promoting numerous campaigns against obersity at a young age showing the detrimental effects that this condition can trig in a life time with the terrific consequences. Moreover, authorities are trying to correct unhealthy habits, taxing sugar in drinks and foods and removing sweet and high fat products from public areas, such as schools and hospitals. Therefore, Government can be consider highly active on confining the increased percentage of overweight children.
 Not only ministries are exstensively advertising health pattern to control weight and discourage consumption of toxic products, but they are also involving the communities in outdoor events, such as presenting the new city soccer team. So that, while promoting sports and active lifestyle, youngster can be influenced by these athletes to pursue a better physical conditions. Nevertheless, government is promptly enhancing the importance of personal training class in primary school offering schoolars the possibility of choosing gym as extra class to add to their curriculum.
 To conclude, although obesity rates are rapidly raising, I do not agree that government is responsible of this outcome. Clearly, it is broadly active engaging children in outdoor activities, yet limiting products from public areas. Hence, authorities are commiting to prevent this plague that could have horrible consequences on future adults.

question: Write about the following topic.Some teachers think that international student exchange would be beneficial for all teenage school students.Do you think its advantages will outweigh the disadvantages?Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.
essay:
Many teachers believe that an international exchange program would benefit all adolescent students. While there are clear drawbacks to this, in my opinion the advantages are more significant. The essay will demonstrate that pupils will build life skills by joining the program. This is followed by explaining why continuing learning in the domestic school after finishing the exchange can be practicable.
 It is obvious that the exchange program is likely to give an opportunity for students to learn to live with people from different society and nationality which is the experience that they are not able to have when studying in their country. As a result, they will have the ability to communicate with various types of people and handle challenging situations. Furthermore, there is research indicating that pupils who study in another country are able to lead a group of acquaintances under pressure.
 On the other hand, those who oppose this may point out that when the exchange complete and the students resume their courses in the domestic school, they are perhaps unable to keep pace with others and, consequently, there might be some students who cannot pass exams. Nevertheless, the aforementioned issue can possibly be addressed by teachers to arrange extra classes for the students in order for them to learn about what they have missed when they attended the exchange program.
 In conclusion, despite the fact that some students may find difficulty in carrying on studies in the school after the completion of the exchange, I am convinced that the benefits outweigh the disadvantages. This is because children are likely to gain valuable experience which they can only have when studying aboard.

question: Write about the following topic.Some universities now offer their courses on the Internet so that people can study online.Is this a positive or negative development?Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.
essay:
Universities are offering many online courses for the convinience of people. Although online courses can never substitute the regular education, some benefits of online courses are undeniable. This essay will discuss both the advantegs and disadvantages of study through the internet.
 Prima facie, education through internet has far more reach than any other method. Universities can approach students from all over the world, even a student from a remote area can be benefitted through the revolutionary internet courses. Online courses are also considered to be cost effective because a student can study from the comfort of home and save lot of living expenses.
 While remote education reaches many students, it lacks in many ways. A physical classroom exeperience gives a student far more learning than the online one. Some practical subjects like Medical Science and Science require a lab facility and presence of students in the classroom environment. Theories can be still taught online but practical education is not feasible. Attending college also provides many mental health benefits, students connects with fellow studenst and teachers and create a social circle which in return result in healthy conversations and meeting. An healthy and happy environment is significant and helps people learn more quickly. Moreover a regular college education comes with many extra curricullar activities like sport, drama, music etc. I can never forget my college days where I enjoyed many youth festivals and sports event which helped in developing overall personality. Colleges provied many other benefit like socail gathering and focus on behaviroul aspect of students as well.
 In a nutshell though online education comes with some good feature but can never replace the physical/regular education system.

question: Write about the following topic.The issue of gay marriage remains controversial. Some people think that this trend has adverse effects on the society while others believe that it is seen as a natural trend and right for people to choose this type of marriage. Should gay marriage be legal?Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.
essay:
There is an argument regarding gay marriage whether this type of marriage will negatively affect on communities, or it is a natural preference and right for everyone. I am of opinion that gay marriage should be legal and the reason will be outlined below.
 To begin with, nowadays, human equality is becoming more and more important. Hence, men should have the right to marry a person with the same gender. Since this right is one of the fundamental living, governments should revise the law to reflect this type of marriage and enforce it accordingly. This is exemplified by developed countries, which pay attention to human rights, allowing a man to marry another man legally.
 Furthermore, gay marriage will not be likely to cause negative effects on societies. On the other hand, this trend tends to increase countriesâ€™ economic growth. As a result of implementing the policy which promote same-sex marriage, gay couple around the world tend to be attracted by this government support, and possibly move and live in the country. To illustrate this, there is research demonstrating that the economy of Canada has been growing substantially after allowing gay marriage to be legal.
 In conclusion, it seems to me that gay marriage should be legal due to the human right and the positive consequent which possibly contribute to the potential rising in countriesâ€™ economy.

question: Write about the following topic.The issue of gay marriage remains controversial. Some people think that this trend has adverse effects on the society while others believe that it is seen as a natural trend and right for people to choose this type of marriage. Should gay marriage be legal?Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.
essay:
The scenario of two people getting married was always welcomed in society but not if those two are of the same sex. This question remains quite arguable currently. A part of the population tends to have the opinion that gay marriage could affect the community in a destructive way, while the other part sees this perspective as the development of social nature, freedoms, and basic human rights. I believe that every individual person has the own will to decide for themselves who they want to marry, overall, other people are not the ones to make a decision.
 First of all, it is widely recognized that conservative and religious societies have a low tolerance for gay marriage because it can destroy their core values and outlook in life. It is generally known that such people think that the legalization of gay marriage can lead to the devaluation of heterosexual couples and their marriage, which is extremely harmful to the community. For example, there is one opinion widely spread in Utah that such legalization can devalue heterosexual marriage, which will lead to an active extramarital sex life, and therefore to a wild increase in the number of abortions. So, it is clear that gay marriage can be adverse.
 On the other hand, forbidding marriages that are not heterosexual equals ignoring the basic rights and freedoms of every person. Besides, the research in Utah shows that the previously mentioned fear is completely irrational and Global Health Organization declared that being homosexual is not one of the mental problems. When couples live together and love each other but can not be bounded by official documents, it can cause a lot of legal problems. For instance, when one of them is in the hospital in critical condition, the other can not make a decision about treating or even visiting a beloved one due to the fact that they are not legally related. Such conditions could lead even to fatal consequences.
 In conclusion, I believe that each individual person can have their own opinion, but this should not affect other people's lives in a negative way. That`s why I think that homosexual marriages should be legalized.

question: Write about the following topic.The issue of gay marriage remains controversial. Some people think that this trend has adverse effects on the society while others believe that it is seen as a natural trend and right for people to choose this type of marriage. Should gay marriage be legal?Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.
essay:
Some people argue that gay marriage it is a trend that might negatively affect society. Controversially, some others believe that same-sex marriages are natural and people have the right to choose this kind of marriage. This essay discusses why gay marriage should not be defined as a trend but regarded as a people's right, thus as a legal marriage.
 During the last few years, several arguments arose against or in favour of same-sex marriage. For decades, people believed that gay people should not be allowed to get married as it was considered against the traditional image of a family. For example, same-sex people wouldn't be able to love and care for a child as a traditional family could. The belief behind this thought is related to the patriarchal educational concept where a child needs to grow in the presence of a mother and a father. Thus, same-sex relations are considered not natural by some people. Considering the definition of love and what caring for someone involves in, isn't indicated that same-sex people cannot feel and express love to each other and care for someone. Love is a feeling that goes beyond the gender. As such, people have the right to love and get married.
 ,
 hence, due to the presence of such a patriarchal belief in most of the world's countries, gay marriage isn't a legal right yet. In a world where people express the need to be more democratic, politically correct and free from biases and discrimination, same-sex marriage should be globally legalised. As such, a change of mindset might be needed. for example, people should begin to see marriage as an expression of love rather than a right and moral action to procreate.
 To sum up, while for some people same-sex marriage is only a trend that should could impact the society, for some others, gay marriage it is a natural expression of love. Thus, marriage should be allowed regardless the gender and people's sexual-orientation. govemrnts. should support and legalised same-sex marriage.

question: Write about the following topic.The issue of gay marriage remains controversial. Some people think that this trend has adverse effects on the society while others believe that it is seen as a natural trend and right for people to choose this type of marriage. Should gay marriage be legal?Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.
essay:
It is a critical topic whether the society should accept gay marriage and legalized this kind of marriage. This especially sensative to some people with religious background or society with strong religious background.
 Some argue that if the society accepts gay marriage, the traditional value and concept of marriage would be affected. The influences are deep and negative.  However, the trend for supporting gay marriage has increased in recent decades, probably because of the equal-right campaign rapidly spreading in the world. question: Write about the following topic.To meet the growing need for food to support an increasing population, a country should make use of edible insects as a food source. However, some people believe that insects are not only unhealthy but harvesting them will also negatively affect nature. What are the benefits and drawbacks of eating insects?Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.
essay:
Among all of global issues, food shortage can clearly be seen as one of the most concerning as the ultimate result of this issue is hunger and death which is relatable to all. One of the solution being brought into spotlight is consuming insects. While some people believe that this can be a proper path to end the world's hunger, others argue that it will further lead new problems.
 To begin with, there are various aspects of advantages in using bugs as protein substitutes. Bugs are not only available in all continents but the majority of them have short life cycles. If farms shift to grow insects rather than the currently available lifestock, less costs and investments will be needed as they only need weeks to mature while other lifestocks require months or even years. Then, what prevent the humanity to covert to insectivore?
 As well as tremendous benefits, the idea of consuming athropods can raise numbers of concerns. Introduction of new insect species and increase of their populations can extremely affect native ecosystem. To elaborate this, alien species introduced from one area to another can be dangerously invasive and can ultimately result in extinction of native species. One example is gold fish which were released into lakes can multiply their populations in short period and over the years, they doubled in size and eradicated their counterparts in original food chain. Therefore, arthropods which are much smaller and aglier can effortlessly escape farms and survive in extreme environment will cause disasters to the nature and crops in the farming areas.
 All in all, consumption of insects can be humanity's new opportunity to survive as it requires less resources and provides abundant produces. Nevertheless, this change in food source require more researches to ensure sanitary and nutritions as well as time for the consumers' adaption to the new concept.

question: Write about the following topic.With the improvements in todayâ€™s health care, society has to care for more and more elderly people.Do you feel that society will be able to cope with the increase in numbers of elderly people today and how can it be managed?Give reasons for your answer and include any relevant examples from your own knowledge or experience.
essay:
As the world has been experiencing rapidly aging population, the issue of providing proper care for the elderly is concerning the society. However, I am inclined to believe that the increase of elderly people will be under control. While many challenges are likely to taken place, there will be effective ways to manage this civil segment with the cooperation of both government and private sectors.
 As the elderly population is expanding, the demand for nursing homes or eldersâ€™ clubs are on the increase. To meet this demand, private sectors are investing on this type of institutions and services as they foresee the lucrative profit these businesses can probably generate in the near future. Apparently, young people are occupied with their daily job which makes it impossible to take care of their parents or grandparents. Therefore, sending the elderly to nursing homes or hiring personal care service are reasonable options to be consider.
 Another reason to validate my opinion is the balance in labor force. While some developed countries are experiencing aging population, the scenario is opposite in developing countries whose birth rate is considerably high. As a result, many developed countries such as Germany have encouraged a welcoming entry of those young laborers into its nursing industry. One of my friend has been settling in Germany following her moving to the country 5 years ago to become a nursing staff in privately-owned elderly clubs.
 Last but not least, government interference in elderly care is highly recommended. It is suggested that the government allocate more budget to establish publicly-owned elderly homes to mitigate the financial burden to acquire this service. Moreover, regulations on pension plan should be properly adjusted and taken more seriously by employers so that people are assured financially when they are retire.
 In conclusion, I believe the elderly problem is likely to be handled well if there are joint effort of both private and public sectors in creating healthcare system and human resource management.

question: Some people think that the teenage years are the happiest time of most people’s lives. Others think that adult life brings more happiness, in spite of greater responsibility. Discuss both views and give your own opinion
essay:
It is often argued that the most enjoyable part of people's lives is their youth; however, other individuals claim that adulthood can be happier. While the latter opinion may hold some truth, I strongly believe that most of a person's cherished memories come from their teenage years.

Some people think that, despite the many obligations and responsibilities that adults face, adulthood is the most joyful phase of life. This perspective is valid because during this period, individuals face new experiences for the first time, which they may not have had during adolescence. Additionally, they create even stronger memories by encountering (synonym of facing) real-world challenges, knowing what is at stake. Furthermore, people over the age of 25 have more freedom to make their own choices without supervision, allowing them to do activities that they weren't previously able to do. For example, traveling abroad with friends or starting a business in various fields can leave a significant impact on their lives and trigger spikes of dopamine in their brains . However, I believe that the joy experienced during adulthood is less than the happiness found in youth.

The happiest period of life for a large portion of people is when they were teenagers. This could be the result of a great number of first-time experiences compared to adulthood. Numerous activities can make teenagers happy, even with supervision. For instance, driving a car for the first time, attending a party, or having a sleepover at a friend's house can be exciting experiences. Moreover, not having responsibilities, besides schoolwork and homework, in other words, the absence of serious real-world problems,  allows teenagers to face life with ease, knowing that the consequences of their actions are often trivial.

In conclusion, while many believe that adulthood is the best period of a person's life, I trust that adolescence is when individuals create their happiest memories.

question: In some countries, the difference in age between parents and children is generally greater than it was in the past. Do you think the advantages outweigh the disadvantages?
essay:
In modern society, people tend to give birth later than in the past. From my perspective, giving birth late would be more beneficial than having children in young age since parents are more stable in their financial situation.

 To begin with, having children when parents are not in their early 20s can provide their children with a better environment. This is mainly because parents have stable careers, so they might support their children more. Therefore, their kids might have extracurricular activities and experience different cultures through international travel with their parents. For example, in South Korea, people have a tendency to give birth after they have job security. They take their kids here and there on their school holidays, and they give their child enough academic support, which leads to better academic achievement. Therefore, children can feel emotional stability and live in an affluent environment

 However, there are several merits of giving birth in one's early 20s. Firstly, parents can give their kids more valuable memories. For example, young parents can play longer, so their kids might feel satisfied, which results in creating precious memories that give mental stability. Another example of this is that parents can be friends with their kids. Young parents  often  follow trendseasily, which makes their conversation between them and their kids smoother. 

 In conclusion, as compared above, I personally believe that although both views have their own merits, the former far outweighs the advantages of the latter due to financial stability and academic support from their parents.

question: Some people argue that because the internet makes it so easy for children to access facts, schools should not focus on teaching facts. Instead, they should focus on developing children’s skills and potential, and their relationships with other people. To what extent do you agree or disagree?
essay:
As information is now so accessible online, it can be argued that schools should move away from fact-based teaching and towards work on skills and relationship building. I completely agree with this idea. In this essay, I will elaborate on my point of view by mentioning two reasons. 

There are two main reasons why I believe that school teachers should no longer focus on presenting factual information to their learners.Firstly,there is simply no need for teachers to create lessons that are already available in multimedia formation the Internet. BiliBili,for example,contains videos that cover the full range of school subjects and lesson topics. Secondly,young people are so adept at using websites and search engines that they can be trusted to do their own online fact-finding. Therefore, basic fact-searching should be done as homework, reserving class time for more valuable activities.  

Furthermore, equipping class with both academic and social capability can lead to more meaningful learning outcomes. To begin with, academic competencies such as research abilities and critical thinking are highly valued in today's workplace. Rather than simply transmitting factual knowledge, teachers should prioritise helping students develop these competence to prepare them for future career success. Similarly, social competencies—including teamwork and strategic problem-solving—are equally essential in professional environments. Without adequate training in these areas during school, students may struggle to meet workplace demands. This evidence strongly suggests that educators should place greater emphasis on strengthening students' overall capabilities rather than focusing solely on content delivery.

In conclusion, I believe that classroom teaching should prioritise skills and interpersonal relationships, while factual knowledge can be studied at home via the Internet.

question: The internet is an amazing invention which has changed the world. Many think for the better while others think it causes problems. Give the advantages and disadvantages of the internet.
essay:
With the advancement of interventions, the internet became one of the human essentials. People in differnt ages are using the internet and need it in their daily life. Even though, the internet has invaded the world in education , health, economic, security and governmental documents. This essay will examine the advantages and disadvantages of the internet on personal and societal levels.

There are many advantages of using the internet. First, ease of communication, which keeps people closer to each other, so people from Australia can have a video call with their family in Kuwait with zero effort. Second, raise family and country income. Based on the last statistics for the Ministry of Commerce in Kuwait , data shows that from every three families , two  have a small online business, which means their expenses are controlled. Third,  it eases the governmental workload. With the use of the internet, many applications and websites are created to help people get their documents and other transactions very easily. By implemnted internet in the organisation, the benefits will be shown in employment performance, fewer errors, fewer efforts, easier to control and offcourse people's satisfaction.

On the other hand, there are disadvantages of using the internet, that threat people's lives. First, there are some neurological issues with the internet. Recent studies in pubmed shown that after the pandamic on 2020 , the level of sezuire raised 20% compared to 2019. Which means, most kids are using iPads and the internet more than 5 hours a day. Second, fraud and stolen data. The war on the internet became about how to keep your data safe from hackers. Ministries made a great effort by setting rules, regulation and raise syperscurity in the whole system. Finally, fewer physical activities. People spend their time on Mobile, laptop and Netflix more than gym or walking, so that they put their bones and muscles at risk thier bones and muscles.

In conclusion, the Internet can help or threaten people's lives based on how people deal with it. The outcome of the internet shows that the benefits are more than the disadvantages. Cybersecurity must be a focus  and raise physical activities in daily life.

question: Some people say that schools are no longer necessary because children can get so much information available through the Internet, and they can study just as well at home. To what extent do you agree or disagree?
essay:
Some people assert that schools are not necessary since children can get a lot of information online and they can also study at home. Is there any merit to this belief? I disagree with this view because although online learning is more accessible and convenient, schools can still provide more direct support from teachers and help students develop social skills.

First of all, online learning can offer better access to learning materials and personalised content. With a computer and an internet connection, children can join online classes and watch a wide range of useful online videos almost anywhere and at any time. Moreover, children can effectively learn academic subjects through personalized content at their own pace. For example, if children are weak at basic numeracy, they can review the calculation at home and keep up with classmates by taking online math courses. Therefore, online learning can help children develop their academic performance with more accessibility.

On the other hand, schools can enhance children's communication skills and provide flexible support from teachers. Through project-style practice and group work, children can learn how to cooperate and collaborate with peers, which can be essential in later life. Furthermore, teachers can provide more practical and flexible feedback depending on each student's situation. By way of example, when children struggle to gain higher scores in English, English teachers can offer one-on-one English classes after school, which can improve their English grades. Consequently, schools are effective in developing children's soft skills and offering teachers' dedicated support.

In conclusion, although online learning can enable children to access a wide variety of learning content more readily, schools can still play a significant role in fostering children's social development and offering teachers’ close guidance. Schools are likely to remain in the future.

question: Some people state that in the period of developed IT, it is normal for teenagers to spend a lot of time on such modern gadgets as smart phones and laptops, while others say that it is a bad habit with negative consequences. Discuss both views and state your opinion.
essay:
In the modern world we live in nowadays, the development of information technology is a topic of public interest and concern. While it is believed by some individuals that it is increasingly common for the younger generations to spend almost all their time on modern devices such as smartphones and laptops, others are of the opinion that investing a large amount of time in these gadgets would come with serious consequences. Personally, I am more supportive of the latter argument. 

On the one hand, there are strong arguments in favor of the notion that there is nothing wrong with adolescents spending a significant amount of time on their mobile phones or their computers. To begin with, nowadays, dedicating a lot of time to using modern devices can bring about certain benefits. First of all, thanks to the increasing of information technology, they can develop themself and broaden their horizon by researching and learning many things online. Hence, modern devices are essential items in humans' lives. Additionally, they are able to catch up on all the news in the world with phones or computers. To make a case for this, the war between Iran and the USA is happening, and teenagers should know this situation and prepare themselves beforehand. Therefore, people can notice this issue without smartphones or laptops. 

On the other hand, there are more convincing reasons to justify the other side of the debate. On top of that, spending too much on technology gadgets will come with serious implications. First and foremost, it will affect their health, especially their eyes. According to BCC news, 80% of young people get short-sighted when they are just 4 or 5 years old, as many parents leave their kids to play games or watch movies on mobile phones for over 180 hours. Furthermore, since they use their phones or their laptops for negative purposes, they perhaps are not able to focus totally on their studies. As a result, they would show poor academic performance.

In conclusion, all things considered, it is evident that although the aforementioned arguments are justified to certain extents, it is more reasonable to say that students will have some drawbacks that are unavoidable if they spend their whole time on modern gadgets.

question: Surveillance cameras in banks, officers, shops and streets have been very successful in reducing crime the workplace and in public, but they are also a tool for their users to spy on people private business-What problems do the use of surveillance cameras create? Is there any solution?
essay:
Monitoring cameras in public places, banks, offices, and shops are effective tools for reducing crime. However, they are also a tool for their users to violate privacy and exploit personal data by intruding on people’s private lives. To address this issue, it is crucial to provide strict laws with regard to spy cameras.

Security cameras lead to a breach of private life. People can feel anxiety and restricted even if they are being watched by someone they know. For example, if children are closely monitored by their mother, they may make more mistakes due to nervousness. And, workers may also feel stressed if their every action is under surveillance.

Additionally,  CCTV can also result in unauthorised use of information.  Recorded footage can be used unethically for commercial gain. For instance, celebrities’ personal information is published by paparazzi using hidden cameras for their own benefit.

Implementing clear regulations and policies by the government can be a good approach for these problems. Authorities should create legislation on where safety cameras can be placed, who can access record, and how it can be used. For example, cameras should be placed only where security is essential , such as in crowded traffic areas, not in private areas like restrooms. Moreover, only administrations can handle and control these observation systems to limit access.

In conclusion, surveillance cameras in banks, offices,shops, and on streets have been very useful in dealing with crimes in public areas.  At the same time, they can also be used to invade personal space and track background information for business profit. These problems can be solved by enforcing strict discipline by the government.

question: Some people claim that public transport should be free of charge and others disagree. Discuss both views and give your opinion.
essay:
Public transportation is widely regarded as the backbone of modern cities, as it provides efficient and affordable mobility for the population. While some argue that it should be made free of charge, others believe that users should pay for such services. This essay will discuss both perspectives before presenting my opinion.

On the one hand, making public transport free could significantly benefit the environment. When commuters are not required to pay, they are more likely to use buses, trams, and trains instead of private vehicles. This shift can lead to reduced traffic congestion and lower carbon emissions. As a result, cities can experience improved air quality and a healthier living environment. For example, urban areas with well-developed public transport systems often report lower pollution levels due to decreased reliance on private cars.

On the other hand, charging fares is essential to sustain public transportation systems. Governments invest substantial financial resources in maintaining infrastructure, upgrading vehicles, and ensuring safety standards. If transport services were made entirely free, authorities might need to increase taxes to cover these costs, which could place a financial burden on citizens. Moreover, the transport sector generates employment for millions of people, and eliminating fares could disrupt revenue streams, potentially affecting job stability.

In conclusion, although free public transport offers environmental advantages, it is not financially sustainable in the long term. I believe that governments should continue charging reasonable fares while offering subsidies or discounted rates to specific groups such as students and senior citizens.

question: in many countries, younger people are forgetting their old and traditional languages. is this accepted? discuss both sides of this issue?
essay:
In many parts of the world, younger generations are gradually losing touch with their traditional languages, often favoring more global forms of communication. While some people consider this shift acceptable in an increasingly interconnected world, others argue that it leads to the erosion of cultural identity. This essay will examine both sides before reaching a reasoned conclusion.

On the one hand, the decline of traditional languages can be viewed as a practical and inevitable development. In today’s globalized society, languages such as English play a crucial role in education, business, and international relations. Young people who focus on widely spoken languages are more likely to access better career opportunities and integrate into the global community. Additionally, having a common language facilitates communication across borders, fostering cooperation and mutual understanding. From this perspective, prioritizing global languages is not only logical but also advantageous.

On the other hand, the loss of traditional languages raises serious cultural concerns. Language is far more than a means of communication; it carries history, values, and identity. When younger generations abandon their native tongues, they risk losing a deep connection with their heritage. Over time, this may result in the disappearance of unique traditions, folklore, and cultural expressions. Moreover, linguistic diversity enriches the world, and its decline could make global culture more uniform and less meaningful.

In conclusion, although the growing dominance of global languages offers clear benefits in terms of communication and opportunity, the disappearance of traditional languages should not be fully accepted. A balanced approach is essential, where young people are encouraged to embrace international languages while still preserving and valuing their linguistic and cultural roots.

question: Billions of dollars are being wasted each year in space research . The money could be better used to improve conditions on eart. To what extent do you agree or disagree with this statement
essay:
In recent years, enormous sums of money have been invested in space exploration, leading some to argue that these funds are wasted and should instead be allocated to solving problems on Earth. While I acknowledge that many pressing issues require immediate attention, I strongly disagree with the idea that space research is a waste of resources.

On the one hand, it is true that the world faces numerous challenges, such as poverty, climate change, and inadequate healthcare systems. Many people believe that governments should prioritize these urgent problems rather than spending billions on space missions. For instance, investing in education, infrastructure, and medical research could significantly improve the quality of life for millions of people. From this perspective, allocating large budgets to space exploration may seem unjustifiable.

On the other hand, space research brings substantial long-term benefits that cannot be overlooked. Firstly, it has led to numerous technological advancements that are now widely used in everyday life, such as satellite communication, GPS systems, and weather forecasting. These innovations have greatly enhanced global connectivity and safety. Secondly, space exploration contributes to scientific knowledge and helps humanity understand the universe better, which may prove crucial for future survival, especially in the face of potential global disasters.

Moreover, investment in space programs can stimulate economic growth and create high-skilled jobs, thereby benefiting society as a whole. Rather than being a waste, such funding can be seen as a strategic investment in the future.

In conclusion, although there are valid concerns about prioritizing resources, I firmly believe that spending on space research is not wasteful. Instead, it plays a vital role in technological progress, economic development, and the long-term survival of humanity.

question: These days many people prefer working at home instead of going to their working place. What are the advantages and disadvantages of this?
essay:
Nowadays, the majority of people tend to work from home instead of going to the office. In this essay, I will tackle the advantages and disadvantages of it.

In this era, where technologies help us to make our lives easier, work is one of them. Other people can work from home if their job is related to technology or can be solved online. The advantages of it are that you can easily finish your task without interruption, and at the same time, you can do minimal house chores while you’re at home. Therefore, people will not get too stressed because they can manage their time well and focus on the things that they need to finish. For instance, less anxiety and no need to worry too much, like waking up early just to go to work and commute every day before going to the office. As well, you can spend more time with your family and friends. 

On the other hand, the disadvantage of working from home is less communication with your peers. Besides working with teammates, need interaction and good team support. For example, if you are at the office, all the members will work hard, and can do a lot of team building where communication skills improve.

To conclude, a lot of people choose to work from home because of the convenience. In particular, where everyone can do their own style, they can finish their job and feel calmer than being at the office, where you can feel the pressure of it.

question: Some people say computers have made life more difficult and complicated, others think that it has made our lives easier. Do you agree or disagree? Give specific reasons and examples to support your view.
essay:
Computers have transformed nearly every aspect of modern life, from communication to education and business. While some argue that this technology has introduced unnecessary complexity into our daily routines, I firmly believe that computers have made our lives significantly easier, more efficient, and considerably more productive.

Admittedly, a segment of society finds computers challenging to use, particularly older generations who did not grow up with this technology. This unfamiliarity can lead to frustration, anxiety, and a strong resistance to adopting new digital tools. For example, many elderly people in developing countries struggle to access basic online services such as banking, healthcare appointments, or government platforms, which creates genuine difficulty in their daily lives. As a result, they perceive computers as a source of complication rather than convenience.

However, for the vast majority of people, computers have dramatically simplified everyday tasks that once consumed enormous amounts of time and effort. Businesses now process financial transactions within seconds, students access vast libraries of knowledge instantly without leaving their homes, and individuals can manage travel bookings, shopping, and personal communication all from a single device. For instance, platforms such as Google and Microsoft Office have completely transformed how professionals work globally, reducing tasks that once took several hours to mere minutes.

In conclusion, while it is entirely understandable that some individuals find computers overwhelming at first, the evidence strongly suggests that technology has made modern life considerably easier and more efficient overall. With proper education, training, and equal access to technology, computers hold the potential to improve the quality of life for all people around the world.

question: Some people say that museums should educate children, others think museums should entertain children instead. Discuss both view and give your opinion
essay:
"Some individuals admit that museums should function only in educational terms for teens, whereas others believe that museums should entertain them instead. From my perspective, completely immersing museums in the educational system repulses youth from themselves. However, museums should not lose their primary goal to spread knowledge.

Turning repositories into study centres might deter people from visiting the site, especially young generations who are accustomed to highly dynamic and dopamine-filled activities.  Them ajority of humans consider studying as a determination, even though it was their desire to gain knowledge, which is the main factor why people are not able to enjoy study sessions. Therefore, turning museums fully into an academic institution discourages people from attending apply museum tours. 

By contrast, making repositories to only function for entertaining purposes is not a fruitful idea either. Changing museums into amusing infrastructures will deprive the original atmosphere that museums provide. The peaceful environments will be replaced with loud noise,and not considering the history in an appropriate way leads to the loss of the long-sought-after philosophy behind them.

From my perspective, neither putting museums into a complete educational path and nor transforming them into entertaining figures is a logical choice. Keeping a balance between is the greatest solution, in which people do not get bored,  while simultaneously receiving knowledge. The best example for this is the well-known museum in France called “Louvre”, which is the perfect fusion of engagement and education,which can be proved by the fame it gained.

In conclusion, museums should not follow the suggestions of either group of people, turning it completely to educational institution or entertaining figure will just put another stain on their reputation. In my opinion, just following the original traditions, which is  educational, is still apply engaging."

question: In some parts of the world, people buy far too many clothes. What are the reasons for this? How can people be persuaded to reduce the number of clothes they buy?
essay:
Nowadays, people buy more clothes than they need in some areas of the world. I completely agree that people should reduce the number of clothes they buy. A discussion of the reasons and how to solve this issue  will be highlighted in the write-up.

One of the main reasons for purchasing too much wearings is items are too cheap. This is because many famous clothing brands are advertising new products every season and manufacture the clothes in a large amount caused a lower price. This trend as known as '' fast-fasion''. Another significant reason is that people are affected by their idols ,TV series or social media. They tend to imitate how celebrities wear. For instance, my friend at the university  is a big fan of K-pop. He wants to look like his idol , so he goes shopping every weekend. Therefore, he bought a lot of cheap clothes , but most of them are in a low-quality. Finally, those items have been thrown out, which is really a waste.

I definitely believe people can reduce the quantity of clothing. Firstly, the government should encourage manufacturers to raise both the quality and price. In this way, products can be used for a longer time and decrease people's shopping desire at the same time. Secondly, celebrities can share their idea of being eco-friendly, and appealing to fans who do not buy too many clothes. Consequently, these methods can persuade people to just buy the wearings which they really need.

In conclusion, I firmly believe that buying too many clothes is not a good sign, which resulted by the low price and idols' fashions. However, this situation can be solved by increasing the quality and price of items and persuading people to stop this behaviour by their idols.

question: in today's world many people own a smartphone. Do you think the advantages of owning a smartphone outweigh the disadvantages?
essay:
Nowadays, smartphones are widely used in daily life. I believe that the advantages of owning a smartphone outweigh the disadvantages.

First of all, smartphones help people stay in touch with their family and friends, which makes them feel less lonely. For instance, people who move to another country can use video calls to communicate with their loved ones when they need emotional support. Social media also allows people to share photos, write posts, and follow updates from friends, which can relieve stress and make them feel more connected to others. Many teenagers even manage their social media accounts independently, which also helps them feel more confident and stylish.

Moreover, smartphones provide many practical applications. People can use apps such as Google Maps to find directions when they get lost, which is much easier than relying on paper maps in the past. Some apps are also useful for elderly people who live alone. For example, certain apps can automatically send an emergency message if the user does not respond for a long time, helping them stay safe and giving their family peace of mind.

Although smartphones can sometimes be distracting, their benefits in communication, safety, and convenience are far more important. In conclusion, owning a smartphone offers numerous advantages that clearly outweigh the disadvantages, especially in modern society where staying connected and accessing information is essential.

question: Many people nowadays spend a large part of their free time using a smartphone. What do you think are the reasons for this? Do you think this is a positive or negative development?
essay:
Nowadays, smartphones have become a key part of daily life. Many people spend a lot of their free time using these devices for various purposes. I believe this is mainly because smartphones offer easy access to entertainment, communication, and information. 

While they have many benefits, using them too much can also cause problems. One major reason people spend so much time on their smartphones is the wide range of entertainment options. Social media platforms, online games, and video apps let users relax and escape from daily stress. Additionally, smartphones make communication very easy. People can send messages, make video calls, and stay connected with friends and family no matter where they are. For instance, many students use messaging apps to talk with classmates or share school materials. As a result, smartphones become a regular part of their daily routine. Moreover, smartphones provide quick access to information and helpful tools. With just a few taps, users can find educational materials, read news, or learn new skills online. Students often depend on their phones to check schedules, set reminders, or study with educational apps. However, spending too much time on a smartphone can lead to negative effects, like less face-to-face interaction or trouble focusing on other activities.

People should learn to manage their time and use technology wisely. In conclusion, smartphones are commonly used during free time because they offer entertainment, easy communication, and quick access to information. I think this trend has both good and bad sides. While these devices can improve daily life and help people stay connected, users need to control how much they use them to avoid becoming too dependent on technology.

question: The average weight of people is increasing and their levels of health and fitness is decreasing. What do you think are the causes of this and suggest some remedies to solve the problem.
essay:
In recent years, there has been a significant increase in the average weight of people, along with a dramatic decline in fitness and overall health levels. This problem has become increasingly common in many countries. This essay will discuss the main causes of this issue and suggest some possible solutions.

One of the main reasons for this problem is that many people neglect physical activity and regular exercise. Modern lifestyles often involve long hours of sitting, with little daily movement such as walking. As a result, this lack of activity can lead to health problems such as diabetes, high blood pressure, and weaker immune systems. Another contributing factor is the growing consumption of junk food instead of healthy and balanced meals. Many individuals also consume more calories than their bodies need, which leads to weight gain and an increased risk of obesity.

To address this issue, several measures can be taken. Firstly, people should adopt a balanced and healthy diet that includes appropriate daily calorie intake. This can help maintain a healthy weight and support the immune system. Secondly, individuals should set fitness goals and engage in regular exercise. For example, walking for at least 30 minutes each day can significantly improve physical health and fitness levels.

In conclusion, unhealthy lifestyles have led to increasing body weight and lower fitness levels in many countries. However, this problem can be addressed by encouraging regular physical activity and promoting healthy eating habits. If these measures are implemented, people's overall health and well-being will improve significantly.

question: Some people believe that professionals, such as doctors and engineers, should be required to work in the country where they did their training. Others believe they should be free to work in another country if they wish. Discuss both these views and give your own opinion.
essay:
Some people believe that professionals such as doctors and engineers should be required to work in the home country where they completed their training. However, others argue that individuals should be free to work abroad. This essay will discuss both views before explaining why I believe professionals should have the freedom to work in another country for personal and professional growth. 

On the one hand, requiring professionals to work in their home country can help address the problem of brain drain. Many developing countries invest significant resources in educating doctors, engines, and other specialists. If these professionals leave immediately after graduation, the country may face a shortage of skilled workers. For example, hospitals and infrastructure projects rely heavily on trained experts to function effectively. Therefore, keeping these professionals in their home country can ensure that citizens receive adequate services and that national development continues. 

On the other hand, allowing professionals to work abroad provides important opportunities for personal and professional growth. In many developed countries, specialists can access advanced technology, better research facilities, and higher salaries. These experiences can help them develop new skills and gain international perspectives. In some cases, professionals may even return to their home country later with valuable knowledge that can benefit local industries and institutions. 

In conclusion, although requiring professionals to remain in their home country may help prevent skill shortages and support national development, individuals should ultimately have the freedom to work abroad. Gaining international experience can contribute to personal growth and may also benefit their home country if they return with valuable knowledge and expertise.

question: In many countries, primary and secondary schools close for two months or more in the summer holidays. What is the value of long school holidays ? What are the arguments in favour of shorter school holidays?
essay:
In many countries, primary and secondary schools close for two months or more during the summer. While such extended breaks are widely believed to benefit young people, others argue that shorter vacations would better support educational progress. Both perspectives present convincing arguments.

One major advantage of long school holidays is that they allow learners to recover from the demands of the academic year. School schedules often involve continuous assignments, examinations and strict deadlines, which can create considerable pressure. A prolonged break therefore provides an opportunity for students to rest both mentally and physically, enabling them to return to their studies with renewed energy and motivation. Moreover, extended vacations create valuable opportunities for family interaction and personal development. During this period, young people may travel, pursue hobbies, participate in sports, or develop practical skills that are rarely explored during the school term.

Nevertheless, there are also persuasive reasons why some people support shorter holidays. A primary concern is the potential loss of knowledge that may occur when learners remain away from academic activities for a long time. After several weeks without studying, many pupils struggle to recall previously learned material. Consequently, teachers are often required to spend additional time reviewing earlier lessons instead of introducing new concepts. In addition, shorter breaks can help maintain consistent learning habits, allowing students to remain academically engaged throughout the year.

In conclusion, although extended school holidays contribute significantly to students’ well-being and personal growth, shorter breaks may help preserve academic continuity and reduce learning loss.
