question: Some people believe that having sport in schools is a waste of time and resources, whilst other people believe that sport in schools is a vital part of education. Discuss both these views and give your opinion
essay:
Sports are becoming more popular and also play an important part in students' lives. Few individuals argue that keeping sports activities in schools is a waste of time and resources, while others oppose this statement , considering sports as an essential aspect in education. This essay will discuss both these views before presenting my opinion.

On the one hand, schools spending money on building arenas for sports can be considered wastage of time and energy. As students can misuse the facility provided by spending more time playing the game, which will directly impact their studies, resulting in poor academic performance. For instance, in many cases, we have witnessed that students are not able to pass in certain subjects, as they spend more time following a sport instead of studying.

However, it also offers certain benefits, making it an important part of education. One Major advantage it offers is that it improves both the physical and mental well-being of students. Indulging in any kind of sports activity can ensure physical fitness and also help to make decisions quickly. For example, regularly following a sport will help a student to stay flexible and also develop skills such as teamwork, communication, decision-making and many more. In addition, having sports alongside  studies will encourage students to be disciplined throughout the year.

To conclude, although having sports in school sometimes can be a waste of resources, its positive aspects must not be ignored. In my opinion, sports are necessary in students' lives for the foundation of a healthy and disciplined lifestyle.

question: Some people think that interview is not reliable method of choosing whom to employ and there are other better methods. To what extent do you agree or disagree.
essay:
In recent years, many people argue that interviews are no longer the best option to select a candidate for a job , and there are many other alternatives for the selection. I strongly agree with this view, as face-to-face interviews bring many complications. In this essay, we will discuss why this is the case and then come to a suitable conclusion.

The main reason why I support this view is that, traditional interviewing method is highly misused  since the global rise in technology. It makes it easier for people to get the knowledge about the interview questions through data without having any hands-on experience. For example, if a person is applying for the role of a team lead at a company, he/she can easily gather the information on various tasks and duties for the role through the internet, and can easily use the same data in the interview to get the job.

Moreover, another important point to consider is that, face-to-face interviews do not help in jobs requiring practical knowledge. For instance, working in a field or in a manufacturing unit will require a lot of experience with handling different kinds of tools and ideas on various materials. Interviewing a candidate for these roles makes it really difficult for employers, as they cannot judge whether they will have any kind of practical knowledge or not. 

In conclusion, although the traditional interviewing method is used by most organisations across the world,  I firmly believe that employers should not just rely on it, and new techniques for selection should be implemented, designing the interview process according to the job duties.

question: The rise of social media has affected personal relationships and society as a whole. Do the advantages of using sociak media for commucation outweigh the disadvantages?
essay:
The rise of social media has significantly influenced personal relationships and society. While some argue that it damages real-life interactions, I believe that the advantages of social media for communication outweigh its disadvantages.

One of the most important benefits of social media is its ability to connect people regardless of geographical distance. In the past, maintaining relationships with friends or relatives living abroad was difficult and expensive. However, platforms such as messaging applications and social networking sites now allow individuals to communicate instantly through text, voice, or video calls. As a result, families and friends can maintain close relationships even when they live far apart. This has been particularly valuable in an increasingly globalized world where people frequently move for education or employment.

Another major advantage is that social media enables the rapid exchange of information and ideas. It allows users to share news, educational materials, and personal experiences with a global audience. For instance, students can collaborate on academic projects, professionals can expand their networks, and communities can raise awareness about important social issues. In this way, social media plays a powerful role in spreading knowledge and encouraging public engagement.

Despite these benefits, social media also has several drawbacks. One significant concern is that excessive use can reduce face-to-face communication and weaken real-life social skills. Some individuals may become overly dependent on online interaction, which can lead to feelings of loneliness or social isolation. Furthermore, social media can sometimes spread misinformation rapidly, which may negatively influence public opinion and decision-making.

Nevertheless, these disadvantages can be minimized through responsible use and better digital literacy. When people use social media thoughtfully and balance online interaction with real-world relationships, the benefits remain substantial.

In conclusion, although social media presents certain challenges such as reduced personal interaction and the spread of misinformation, its ability to connect people globally and facilitate communication makes it an invaluable tool in modern society. Therefore, the advantages of social media clearly outweigh the disadvantages.

question: Should marketing in companies which produce consumer goods, like food and clothing, place emphasis on the reputation of the company or short-term strategies like the discounts and special offers? Why?
essay:
Companies that produce consumer goods such as food and clothing must decide how to promote their products effectively. Some people believe that companies should focus on building a strong reputation, while others think short-term strategies such as discounts and special offers are more important. In my opinion, although discounts can attract customers quickly, companies should place greater emphasis on building a strong reputation.

First, a good reputation helps companies gain long-term trust from customers. When people believe that a brand offers high-quality and reliable products, they are more likely to buy from that company again. For example, many well-known clothing and food brands maintain loyal customers because people trust the quality and safety of their products. This trust helps companies achieve long-term success.

Second, reputation can create a strong brand image. Companies that focus on product quality, good customer service, and honesty in marketing can stand out in a competitive market. As a result, customers may choose their products even if the prices are slightly higher than others.

However, short-term strategies such as discounts and special promotions also have some benefits. These strategies can attract new customers and increase sales in a short period of time. For instance, supermarkets often offer discounts to encourage people to try new products or buy more items.

Despite these advantages, relying too much on discounts may reduce the perceived value of a product and may not create customer loyalty. People might only buy the product when the price is reduced.

In conclusion, while short-term promotions can increase sales quickly, companies should mainly focus on building a strong reputation because it helps create trust, loyalty, and long-term success.

question: Nowadays, a growing number of people with health problems are trying alternative medicines and treatments instead of visiting their usual doctor. Do you think this is a positive or a negative development?
essay:
There is no denying the fact that diseases need to be cured by a profession. It is a commonly held belief that individuals with health complications are trying other options rather than visiting an expert, such as alternative medicines and treatments. Does this development consider a positive or a negative? This essay will discuss both points of view and express my opinion.

On the one hand, most members of society prefer to use an alternative way for curing diseases, due to its low cost. In other words, curing yourself through the use of herbs and other old ways from your mother, would significantly reduce the financial expenditure on medicine drug and hospitalisation. In addition, alternative treatment methods are more accessible for some individuals , especially for those who live in rural areas. For example, if you are living in a village, which is far from hospitals, you will depend on an alternative way to cure yourself.

On the other hand, a considerable number of people do not actually benefit from this kind of treatment, which contributes to elevating the risk of death. It is also possible to say that if you had a major injury in your body, such as an infection or inflammation, you would need to visit a real physician. Moreover, using an alternative way instead of going to a healthcare provider would substantially increase the risk of disease transmission. For instance, recent studies have shown that Somalis’ individual health has a substantial vulnerability to infection, as a result of relying on alternative treatments, rather than visiting a doctor.

In conclusion, there are no easy answers to this question . On balance, however, I believe that the disadvantages of this development outweigh the advantages, and regardless of the circumstances, we should visit a doctor seeking  treatment.

question: Some people believe that schools should focus mainly on academic subjects such as mathematics and science. Others believe that subjects such as music, art, and sports are equally important. Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
essay: In modern times, tertiary education plays a crucial role in individuals' lives and contributes to the development of society. While some people argue that schools should focus mainly on academic subjects such as mathematics and science, others believe that subjects like music, art, and sports are equally important. This essay will discuss both perspectives before presenting my own view. On the one hand, the goal of the education system is to facilitate lifelong learning and improve people's quality of life. Through a curriculum that includes a broad range of subjects, pupils acquire not only theoretical knowledge but also essential skills for future, such as critical thinking, problem-solving, independent learning, and analytical and cognitive abilities. If these subjects are given equal importance, they can form a strong foundation for success in many aspects of life. For instance, while mathematics and science enhance intellectual development and logical reasoning, music, art, and sports education play a vital role in emotional well-being, fostering creativity, building self-confidence, promoting physical health, and aiding stress management, in addition to enhancing artistic ability. On the other hand, not only do academic subjects enhance intellectual development, but they also contribute to technological advancement. These disciplines are fundamental to technological and scientific progress, equipping individuals with problem-solving abilities that are essential for innovation. Moreover, strong foundations in fields such as medicine, engineering, and information technology often lead to highly sought-after and well-paid careers, which can significantly improve individuals’ career prospects and contribute to overall economic growth. In conclusion, it is widely acknowledged that all subjects are essential for both career prospects and societal development. A well-designed curriculum can greatly enhance technological and economic progress while also fostering creativity, social understanding, and a healthy and fulfilling life. From my perspective, students should receive a balanced education in which all subjects are given equal importance.

question: Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of online learning compared to traditional in-class education. Which method do you think is more effective and why?
essay:
In recent years, there has been an ongoing debate regarding how technology has significantly impacted education. A group of individuals believe that using digital learning platforms brings numerous benefits. However, others argue that it creates challenges in the classroom. Therefore, before stating my opinion, both views will be discussed further. I would like to shed light on both viewpoints.

Examining the former opinion, the primary argument the supporters would forward is that technology allows for flexible learning schedules with virtual tools. Online tools such as Zoom or Google help students collaborate from different locations. The reason is that it makes learning more accessible and effortless. Students can study whenever they want and don't have to go to a real classroom. With apps like Zoom, they can easily talk and work with their classmates from home. In addition, they also believe that interactive tools like smartboards make the lessons more engaging and dynamic. This is because the creation of engaging and vibrant lesson designs makes learning more enjoyable, which encourages students to be more proactive in their studies.

On the contrary, those in favor of the latter opinion have their own arguments. To begin with, they claim that assessing students' progress fairly with online tools is challenging due to cheating on the exams. For example, some pupils tend to use AI tools such as Gemini or ChatGPT to generate entire essays or solve complex math problems without actually understanding the underlying concepts. Moreover, learners can be severely distracted by entertainment platforms such as TikTok and Facebook, leading to a decline in academic performance and a knowledge gap compared to their peers.

To conclude, there are convincing arguments both for and against the use of technology in education. It is a double-edged sword, offering immense opportunities for learning while also presenting significant challenges. However, I certainly favor the former opinion.

question: In many countries, people are working longer hours than before. Why is this happening? What problems can this cause for individuals and families?
essay:
In the ultra-modern era, working more than regular hours has become a necessity for individuals. There is a wide array of reasons that create this problem. Working excessively can also be harmful to individuals. The upcoming paragraphs will shed light on the reasons and the problems associated with this phenomenon.

To commence with, there are a plethora of reasons why people are working extra hours nowadays. The first and foremost reason is the increased inflation in every country. As prices of almost everything , such as groceries, petrol, clothing and other basic things, are increasing therefore, extra money is needed to cope with this inflation. Hence,  people have to work extra hours to cover all the expenses. Secondly, as the standard of living is increasing day by day. So, to maintain high living standards in society, it has become important for families to earn more. As a result, they have to work overtime.

Despite these reasons, working longer hours also creates several problems. The most significant issue is that people are not able to give enough time to their kids and family. As working individuals are spending more time at work rather than at home, due to this the bonding and love between them is decreasing. Thus, the family ties are getting weaker. Moreover, most of the people follow a sedentary lifestyle , and it is affecting their bodies and health. For example, people working in offices are always sitting and working on a computer. Owing to this, they usually suffer from back pain, poor eyesight, obesity and other health issues. Consequently, it decreases their chances of living a longer and healthier life.

To conclude, individuals have no other option than working extra hours due to increased expenses and high living standards. However, the side effects of working overtime cannot be neglected.

question: some believe that government should provide free health care.However some think that government may not able to provide health care and people should pat for it. what do you think?
essay:
There is an ongoing debate about whether governments should provide free healthcare or whether individuals should bear the cost themselves. While some argue that citizens should pay for medical services, I firmly believe that healthcare is a fundamental right and the government should ensure it is free for everyone.

One of the most compelling reasons for free healthcare is that it promotes equality and social welfare. Not all individuals can afford expensive medical treatment, and without government support, poor and vulnerable populations may suffer or even die from preventable illnesses. For example, countries like Canada and the United Kingdom provide universal healthcare, ensuring that everyone, regardless of income, has access to essential medical services. This approach reduces inequality and helps build a healthier and more productive society.

Moreover, government-funded healthcare can prevent larger economic costs in the long term. When people are able to access preventive care and early treatment without worrying about expenses, the incidence of serious diseases decreases. This reduces hospital admissions and lowers the burden on the economy. For instance, immunization programs funded by the government not only save lives but also reduce future costs associated with treating widespread infectious diseases.

However, opponents argue that free healthcare may strain government resources, especially in developing countries with limited budgets. They claim that individuals should contribute to avoid overuse of services and ensure sustainability. While this concern is valid, it can be mitigated through effective policies such as prioritizing essential services, implementing public-private partnerships, or introducing minimal co-payments for non-critical services.

In conclusion, although providing free healthcare may require careful planning and resource allocation, I strongly believe that it is the government’s responsibility to ensure that citizens have access to medical care. Guaranteeing free healthcare not only upholds social justice but also contributes to a healthier and more prosperous society.

question: Some people believe that the design of buildings and public spaces should prioritize functionality over appearance. Others think that aesthetics are equally important. Discuss both views and give your opinion
essay:
The debate between functionality and aesthetics in architecture and urban design involves fundamental questions about how built environments should serve human needs. While functionality is essential, I believe that beauty and visual appeal are equally important for creating spaces where people truly flourish. 

Proponents of prioritizing functionality argue that buildings and public spaces exist primarily to serve practical purposes. Homes must provide shelter, offices need efficient workspaces, and roads must facilitate movement. When functionality drives design, resources are used efficiently, and spaces work effectively for their intended purposes. A purely functional hospital, for example, allows medical staff to work efficiently and patients to receive care without unnecessary complications. In this view, appearance is secondary to how well a space performs its core functions. 

However, advocates for aesthetics point out that humans are not purely practical creatures. Beautiful spaces affect mood, wellbeing, and quality of life in measurable ways. People linger longer in attractive public squares, fostering community interaction. Employees in well-designed workplaces report higher satisfaction and productivity. Patients in hospitals with natural light and pleasant views recover faster. Architecture shapes experience, and beauty contributes to human flourishing just as functionality does. Furthermore, the distinction between function and aesthetics can be false. Well-designed spaces integrate both. A park that is both beautiful and functional encourages exercise and social connection. A school that inspires through its designcan enhance learning. The most successful buildings serve practical purposes while also lifting spirits and creating meaning. 

In my view, the goal should be thoughtful integration rather than choosing one over the other. Designers should consider how spaces will be used while also seeking to create beauty that enriches daily life. This requires collaboration between architects, urban planners, and communities to ensure that built environments serve both practical and human needs. 

Ultimately, we deserve spaces that work well and feel good. Functionality without beauty creates efficient but soulless environments, while beauty without function creates pleasant but impractical spaces. The best design achieves both.

question: Many people believe that international tourism has negative implications on their countries. What is the reason for it? What can be done to change the negative attitude towads international tourism?
essay:
When we look around the world, tourism on a global scale is not always viewed positively from people’s perspectives, especially in regions where communities are dominated by conservative social values. Many residents believe that international tourism may threaten their traditional lifestyles and cultural identity. One major reason for this concern is cultural influence. The arrival of large numbers of foreign visitors often introduces different customs, clothing styles, and social behaviors that may conflict with local norms. As a result, some communities fear that younger generations might gradually abandon their cultural traditions in favor of foreign practices.

Another significant factor is the environmental and social pressure associated with mass tourism. Popular tourist destinations frequently experience overcrowding, increased waste generation, and the overexploitation of natural resources. Moreover, the economic benefits of tourism are not always distributed equally. In many cases, multinational companies and large tourism businesses gain most of the profits, while local residents receive limited financial advantages. This situation can create frustration and strengthen negative attitudes toward international visitors.

To address these concerns, governments and tourism authorities should promote sustainable and community-based tourism strategies. Local communities must be actively involved in tourism planning and decision-making so that development respects their cultural values and social structures. Additionally, strict environmental regulations should be implemented to reduce pollution and protect natural resources. Educational campaigns can also highlight the benefits of tourism, such as employment opportunities, cultural exchange, and improved infrastructure. By ensuring that tourism development is responsible and inclusive, countries can gradually change public perceptions and transform international tourism into a mutually beneficial experience for both visitors and host communities.

question: Some people think only the best students should be rewarded with academic distinctions; others think we should reward weaker students who make progress. Discuss both views and give your opinion.
essay:
Whether academic rewards should be reserved for top students or also given to weaker students who show improvement is a debated topic in education. I believe both types of students deserve recognition, but the awards should be designed differently.

Those who support rewarding only the best students argue that academic honours should be a fair return for ability and hard work. If everyone receives an award, its value is diluted and it loses its motivating power. In Taiwan, for example, the class merit award is given only to top-ranking students, and this competitive system does push students to study harder and prepares them mentally for the realities of workplace competition in the future.

However, focusing exclusively on top performers ignores the effort of the majority. A student who improves from 30 to 70 marks may have worked far harder than one who maintains a score of 90 to 95, yet if such progress receives no recognition, struggling students can easily lose motivation and give up entirely. Research has shown that students who receive encouragement for their improvement are more likely to develop a long-term habit of learning. A more effective approach would be to create different categories of awards. Schools could offer an academic excellence award for outstanding results alongside a best improvement award for significant progress. This way, high academic standards are maintained while all students feel that their effort is acknowledged and valued.

In conclusion, both excellence and effort deserve recognition. Schools should design a diverse reward system that motivates top achievers to maintain their standards while also encouraging weaker students to keep striving for improvement.

question: Many customs and traditional ways of behaviour are no longer relevant to modern life and not worth keeping. Do you agree or disagree?
essay:
Many people argue that customs and traditional behaviours are becoming irrelevant in modern life and therefore should not be preserved. I partly agree, while some traditions are worth preserving for cultural continuity, many outdated customs should be removed because they harm individuals and society. In this essay, I will explain both sides and show why more harmful traditions should be abandoned.

Some traditional practices, such as respecting parents and wearing national dress on festivals, still offer important cultural and familial benefits. Young people today often value independence, which can lead them to neglect traditional family obligations. For example, some Gen Z members believe they can live as they wish without informing or caring for their parents, weakening family ties. As a result, neglecting family duties can foster selfishness and social isolation, reducing mutual support among relatives. This emotional isolation may harm mental health because individuals cannot share problems or receive emotional support from family. Modern youth often express identity through appearance, such as clothes and hairstyles, which can reduce interest in traditional attire. Consequently, many prefer contemporary fashion and consider traditional dress old-fashioned and impractical. If this trend continues, younger generations may lose connection to national cultural heritage as customs stop being practiced.

However, some traditional rules impose modern burdens and therefore deserve reconsideration or removal. In some Asian cultures, families pressure women to bear sons, causing gender discrimination and social harm. For example, China’s one-child policy historically intensified son preference and social imbalances. As a result, some families resorted to illegal measures to secure a male child, worsening gender inequality. Traditional views that women should stay home restrict their opportunities for education and careers. This belief should be challenged because it prevents many women from achieving personal and professional development. Many women have the right to work and pursue ambitions, not only to perform household duties. Consequently, such traditions can destroy women’s prospects, limiting economic independence and life choices.

In conclusion, while some traditions deserve protection, I strongly believe that many outdated customs should be abandoned because they harm individuals and society.

question: The prevention of health problems and illness is more important than treatment and medicine. Government funding should reflect this. To what extent do you agree?
essay:
Among medical researchers, the proper approach to health has been discussed for many years. Nowadays, it goes without saying that it is better to prevent health issues than treat them. I partially agree with this statement.

The importance of prevention dates back to the discovery of the first vaccines against illnesses such as polio and influenza, which enhanced people’s immunity and life expectancy. Once vaccines became widely available, more individuals were able to fight severe medical conditions and considerably improve their quality of life. For example, in the past only one child out of five was likely to survive in good physical condition. Thanks to vaccination programmes, children today are about 40% more likely to survive to their next birthday compared with a scenario without vaccines, showing the dramatic impact of immunisation.

Furthermore, authorities should allocate public funds to social campaigns to raise awareness of prevention. The more authorities encourage residents to have regular check-ups, exercise, and maintain a balanced diet, the better. Scandinavian nations are good examples of this trend, as many families take part in activities such as cross-country skiing. Recent research shows that in some Scandinavian nations the average number of medical consultations has decreased by about 12% over the last five years.

However, even though many preventive solutions exist, some health concerns require medical intervention. Advanced treatments such as long-term chemotherapy for severe illnesses like cancer or modern genetic therapies are indispensable. A balanced healthcare system therefore requires both prevention and treatment to deal with major illnesses and possible outbreaks of infectious disorders.

To sum up, although prevention can help societies maintain better well-being and reduce illnesses, it is equally important to provide proper medical treatment. Only a balanced approach can ensure a high standard of healthcare for the entire population.

question: Many people say that the only way to guarantee a good job is to complete a course of university education. Others claim that it is better to start work after school and gain experience in the world of work. Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
essay:
It is argued that tertiary-level courses are beneficial for securing a lucrative position, while others argue that it is wise to work after higher secondary school to gain job-related skills and knowledge. While university education leads to higher wages and fulfilment, I believe it is better to opt for work-related opportunities after the twelfth standard, as it prepares teenagers for challenges in the professional world.

On the one hand, it is evident that completing advanced academic studies opens up a world of highly paid employment. This implies that enhanced knowledge provides an individual with countless opportunities to earn well. It should be noted that, with the ever-rising inflation, this financial stability contributes to the overall mental well-being of the individual and their family. Research reveals that graduates from many reputable universities end up working for firms such as JPMorgan Chase, thereby emphasising that education is very important for career development. Moreover, these high achievers eventually get international recognition through cross-cultural campaigns. This shows that studies carried out after high school improve career prospects and quality of life.

On the other hand, starting part-time or full-time work after finishing school can help students develop practical skills. In other words, such experiences help polish skills such as communication, time management, and problem-solving. It is worth noting that the development of these competencies leads to reduced work-linked anxiety. For instance, a survey found that youth who engaged in entry-level paid jobs prior to their professional roles were able to meet the company’s goals with minimal resistance. Considering this, engaging in voluntary works makes young more confident and capable, thereby increasing their potential for professional excellence

To conclude, although obtaining a professional degree leads to better job placements, it seems to me that developing capabilities through apprenticeships plays a crucial role in addressing workforce issues.

question: Some people believe that governments should ban dangerous sports. Others claim that they should have the freedom to choose their favourite activities. Discuss both views and present your opinion
essay:
It is argued that establishments must disallow harmful leisure activities, while others argue that people should be free to choose their most liked activities.  Although engaging in unsafe sports would increase the incidence of casualties, I believe that individuals must be given the opportunity to play their favourite sports, as it leads to fulfilment.

On one hand,it is evident that playing potentially harmful outdoor activities would lead to accidents. This implies that there is a higher possibility of people getting head injuries. It should be noted that the resultant injuries then have a detrimental effect on people’s lives. Research shows that a majority of relay racers end up getting injured, and the life-threatening scenario leaves them crippled for life. Secondly, the recovery is extremely slow and involves expensive treatments. This shows the significance of the discontinuity of unsafe sports.

On the other hand, the ability to participate in an activity of personal preference promises a fulfilling experience. In other words,when an athlete favours playing a particular sport,it boosts their confidence. It is worth noting that by adopting this approach, many excel to become famous sportsmen. For instance, the famous Pakistani cricketer Waqar Younis polished his bowling technique through practice sessions in his own neighbourhood. Moreover, many sportsmen achieve global recognition by refining their innate talent in their area of interest. Considering this, when players are given the liberty to choose their preferred pursuits, they positively impact both the individual and society, which I believe plays a crucial role.

To conclude, although athletics might lead to unlikely consequences, it seems to me that in order to promote the well-being of the individual and society, it is more appropriate if people are permitted to decide.

question: Some children spend hours everyday on their smartphones. Why is this the case ? Do you think this is a positive or negative developments?
essay:
The widespread use of smartphones among children has become a growing concern, as many of them spend several hours each day using these devices. This trend is mainly driven by technological accessibility and changing lifestyles. While smartphones can provide certain educational benefits, I believe that this development is largely negative due to its harmful effects on children’s health, behavior, and social skills.

One of the main reasons children spend long hours on smartphones is the rapid expansion of digital entertainment. Modern smartphones provide easy access to games, videos, and social media platforms that are specifically designed to capture users’ attention for long periods of time. As a result, children often become absorbed in these activities, sometimes to the point of addiction. In addition, many parents allow their children to use smartphones as a convenient way to keep them occupied while they are busy with work or other responsibilities. This practice unintentionally encourages children to rely heavily on digital devices for entertainment.

Another contributing factor is the increasing role of technology in education and communication. Many schools incorporate digital tools and online platforms into learning, which requires children to use smartphones or other devices. Furthermore, children use messaging applications to communicate with friends and classmates, making smartphones an essential part of their daily social lives.

Despite these advantages, I believe this trend is largely a negative development. Excessive smartphone use can lead to a variety of physical and mental health problems. For instance, prolonged screen time is associated with eye strain, poor sleep patterns, and reduced physical activity. Moreover, spending too much time on smartphones may hinder children’s ability to develop strong social and communication skills, as they have fewer opportunities for face-to-face interaction. Over time, this may lead to social isolation and weaker interpersonal relationships.

In conclusion, although children spend many hours on smartphones due to easy access to digital entertainment and the growing role of technology in education and communication, the overall impact is mostly negative. Excessive smartphone use can harm children’s physical health and social development, making it a concerning trend that parents and educators should carefully manage.

question: Although parents are responsible for raising a child, outside influences play a key role in their development. To what extent do you agree or disagree with this statement? Give your own opinion.
essay:
I largely agree that although parents are primarily responsible for raising a child, outside influences also play a crucial role in shaping their development.

Parents are usually the first and most important role models in a child’s life. From an early age, they teach basic values, social behavior, and moral principles. For example, children often learn how to communicate, respect others, and deal with challenges through observing their parents. In addition, parents provide emotional support and guidance, which helps children develop confidence and a sense of security. Without this strong foundation, it may be difficult for children to grow into responsible and well-adjusted adults.

However, as children grow older, external factors begin to have a significant impact on their attitudes and behavior. One of the most influential factors is the school environment. Teachers and classmates can shape a child’s academic interests, social skills, and worldview. For instance, supportive teachers can inspire students to pursue their talents, while friendships can influence hobbies, language, and lifestyle choices. Moreover, modern technology and media also expose children to different cultures, ideas, and information that may shape their perspectives beyond what they learn at home.

Another important influence is the wider community. Participation in sports teams, clubs, and cultural activities helps children develop teamwork, discipline, and independence. These experiences allow them to interact with people from different backgrounds and learn practical life skills that parents alone cannot fully provide.

In conclusion, while parents play the central role in raising children and laying the foundation for their character, outside influences such as school, peers, and society also significantly contribute to their overall development. Therefore, I strongly agree that a child’s growth is shaped by both parental guidance and external factors

question: Discuss advantage and disadvantage of online learning compared to traditional in pearson education
essay:
In recent decades, online learning has emerged as a popular alternative to traditional face-to-face education. While this method of learning offers several significant advantages, it also presents certain challenges. This essay will examine both the benefits and the drawbacks of online education compared with conventional classroom learning.

One of the most notable advantages of online learning is the flexibility it provides. Students are able to access lectures and course materials at any time and from virtually any location, allowing them to organize their study schedules more efficiently. This is particularly advantageous for individuals who must balance education with professional or family commitments. Furthermore, online learning platforms often provide a wide variety of digital resources, including recorded lectures, online databases, and interactive learning tools. These resources enable students to review complex topics repeatedly, which can significantly enhance their understanding and overall academic performance.

Despite these benefits, online learning also has several notable disadvantages. One major drawback is the limited face-to-face interaction between students and instructors. In traditional classroom settings, learners can immediately ask questions, participate in discussions, and engage in collaborative activities that deepen their understanding of the subject. Although online platforms attempt to replicate this interaction through video conferencing and discussion forums, these methods are often less effective than direct communication. In addition, online learning requires a high level of self-discipline and motivation. Without the structured environment of a physical classroom, some students may struggle to remain focused and manage their time effectively.

In conclusion, although online learning offers considerable benefits such as flexibility and access to diverse learning resources, it also presents challenges related to limited interaction and the need for strong self-discipline. Therefore, a balanced approach that combines both online and traditional methods may provide the most effective educational experience.

question: Students having part-time jobs Advantages & Disadvantages Essay
essay:
In many countries, it has become increasingly common for students to take part-time jobs while continuing their studies. While this trend offers a number of benefits, it may also create certain challenges for students. This essay will examine both the advantages and disadvantages of students engaging in part-time employment.

One significant advantage of having a part-time job is that it allows students to gain financial independence. By earning their own income, students can cover personal expenses such as transportation, study materials, and leisure activities, which reduces the financial pressure on their families. Moreover, part-time employment provides valuable work experience that cannot always be obtained in the classroom. Through interacting with colleagues and customers, students develop essential skills such as communication, teamwork, and problem-solving. These abilities are highly valued by employers and can significantly improve students’ career prospects after graduation.

Despite these benefits, there are also some drawbacks associated with working while studying. One major disadvantage is that part-time jobs may distract students from their academic responsibilities. Students who work long hours often have less time to complete assignments or prepare for examinations, which may negatively affect their academic performance. In addition, balancing work and study can lead to physical and mental fatigue. For instance, students who work evening shifts may feel exhausted during lectures the following day, making it difficult for them to concentrate effectively.

In conclusion, although part-time jobs can help students develop financial independence and practical skills, they may also interfere with academic success if not managed carefully. Therefore, students should ensure that their work commitments do not compromise their primary goal of achieving a quality education

question: A few languages are increasingly spoken in different countries, while the use of others is rapidly declining. Is it a positive or negative development?
essay:
These days, there is a tendency that a small number of languages have gained widespread
 popularity in the world, while many others are being less spoken. In my opinion, this
 trend could bring both positive and negative consequences.
 To begin with, there are substantial benefits to society when many countries share
 common languages. The first benefit is that cross-cultural communication can become
 easier nowadays, which helps boost multilateral trade and cultural exchange. It is
 true that ones who are bilingual or multilingual find it easier to do business or work
 in international companies as their issues at work can be addressed and discussed
 thoroughly. Furthermore, by acquiring languages which are commonly used in business,
 science and technology, people from less developed countries can accelerate the
 process of learning from more developed countries. For example, India achieves such
 incredible growth partly by using English as their official language.
 Despite the great convenience of sharing common languages, the fact that other
 languages are less spoken can bring several drawbacks. The first disadvantage is that
 communities would lose parts of their cultural traditions such as songs, myths and
 poetry which are not easily translated into another language. This may in turn affect
 their cultural identity, producing a weakened social cohesion as their values and
 traditions are replaced with new ones. Moreover, this trend affects not only the local
 culture within one nation but also the civilization of mankind. If all countries no longer
 use their own language but use the same one, this could gradually destroy the cultural
 diversity of the world.
 In conclusion, although I believe the trend is inevitable in the era of globalisation, people
 need to have measures to protect their less spoken languages as a way to preserve their
 cultural identity.

question:
Although more and more people read the news on the Internet, newspapers will 
 remain the most important source of news for the majority of people. Do you agree 
 or disagree?
essay:
Some people believe that despite the spreading popularity of the online media,
 traditional newspaper will still hold the most vital position among news readers. In my
 opinion, this idea is rather absurd as the Internet has become increasingly dominant
 due to the benefits it offers.
 The first thing that attracts people to reading news on the online sources is their easy
 access. With the presence of the Internet around almost every corner of the world,
 people with a portable device such as a mobile phone can access huge storage of news.
 In fact, latest events, for example IS Terrorist attacks, would be updated immediately on
 CNN or New York Times. By contrast, with prolonging procedures including editing and
 printing, traditional newspaper might not be able to compete with the Internet in terms
 of the updating speed.
 Another benefit with which the Internet can, from my point of view, take over the
 position of printed newspaper is the new experience it brings to readers. One limitation
 of newspaper is that it cannot convey visual messages to the people who read news on
 a regular basis. Meanwhile, non-stop progress of the technology industry has unfolded
 the possibility to upload videos and voice records on various online websites. For
 instance, soccer fans these days can rewatch their favorite matches anytime they want
 with only a laptop connected to the Internet, which a mere paper cannot do.
 In conclusion, I disagree that printed newspaper would continue to be the crucial source
 of information because more and more people will be drawn to use the Internet.

essay:

question:
Cyclists and car drivers sharing the same road might cause some problems. What are the
 problems? What could be done to solve those problems?
essay:
Nowadays, it is common to see cars and bicycles travel on
 the same road. This could have a number of undesirable
 consequences(1) which could be addressed with several different
 approaches.
 When automobiles and bicycles have to share the same road
 two main problems can occur. Firstly, the possibility of an
 accident between a car and a bicycle is much higher when
 they travel on the same road together, and the mortality rate(2)
 of cyclists involved in accidents with cars is significantly
 high. Because most automobiles are comparatively large and
 operate at high speeds(3), it is easy for serious injuries to occur
 when they collide with(4) cyclists, usually far more serious than
 accidents occurring between cyclists. Secondly, there are many
 inconveniences for those driving cars when sharing the same
 road with cyclists. For instance, automobiles may get held up in
 traffic jams due to the slow speed that cyclists travel at. This is
 a very common problem that can be seen in many Asian cities
 where a lot of poor citizens still have to use bicycles for their
 transportation.
 In order to deal with such problems, there are two solutions that
 should be taken into consideration(5). First of all, on roadways
 that allow cars and bicycles to travel together, there should
 be strict speed limits(6), as low as 50 km/h. While moving at
 such low speeds, drivers can better control their vehicle, and
 in the event of a collision, the damage will be significantly less
 serious. Secondly, it is necessary for the government to build
 separate lanes for these two modes of transport so that their
 significantly different speeds will not bother each other and
 cause traffic jams. For example, on most streets in Singapore,
 automobiles and bikes are able to travel simultaneously(7), but
 there are lanes designated for(8) both of them, so they are able to
 go at an appropriate speed without affecting one another.
 In conclusion, when cyclists and automobile drivers share the
 same road, several issues may arise. Fortunately, two possible
 methods have been proposed to solve these issues.

question:
Employers nowadays put more emphasis on social skills. Some people believe that social skills are
 more important in addition to good qualifications for a job success. To what extent do you agree
 or disagree?
essay:
These days, a growing number of recruiters are targeting
 applicants with good people skills as many people believe
 that they are just as important as formal qualifications. In my
 opinion, I firmly agree that possessing good social skills, apart
 from academic skills(1), can give people an edge in advancing
 their career.(2)
 Undeniably, formal qualifications play a critical role in one’s
 career because they are generally good indicators of a person’s
 capability and specialized knowledge. In fact, all employers
 treasure the intellectual power of their employees, as it is a
 valuable asset that can help a company thrive. This is particularly
 true for works that involve in-depth research and development
 such as information technology, computer science, and
 medicine. Without technical knowledge, a person stands little chance of being hired, let alone getting a promotion.(3)
 However, having only stellar credentials(4) does not always
 guarantee one’s career success. In this modern society, people
 rarely work individually and often need to rely on others to get
 the best result. For that reason, people who have excellent
 interpersonal skills(5) and the ability to collaborate(6) are more
 likely to build good relations with their colleagues and perform
 better at work. In contrast, those who lack such critical skills
 may suffer from isolation or miscommunication and trigger
 conflict(7)among workers, hindering the overall working process.
 In short, people who possess good communication skills can
 often gain the love and trust of their co-workers, and stand a
 higher chance of climbing the career ladder.(8)
 In conclusion, I believe that social skills have a strong correlation
 with career success as they can not only help a person integrate
 but also facilitate their working process.

question: Environmental protection should be the responsibility of politicians, not individuals as individuals can do too little. To what extent do you agree or disagree?
essay:
Some people think that politicians should be responsible for protecting the environment
 as there is nothing much that individuals can do about this problem. In my opinion,
 the responsibility to protect the environment should not fall upon politicians alone
 because ordinary citizens can make a significant contribution.
 Firstly, politicians can urge the government to impose new laws against actions that
 damage the environment. For instance, one of the major factors leading to environmental
 pollution is the overuse of plastic products, like bottles and bags, and this can be stopped
 if the government issues an official ban on all companies from using plastic packaging.
 In addition, the rate of deforestation can also be reduced if high-ranking bureaucrats
 agree to impose strict punishments, such as long-term imprisonment and heavy fines,
 on those who cut down trees illegally. However, besides introducing and enforcing new
 laws and regulations, I doubt that there is any further action that politicians can take to
 protect the environment.
 On the other hand, I believe that ordinary people, through small, everyday actions, can
 also greatly contribute to protecting the environment. First, citizens in many countries,
 like the Netherlands, have now shifted towards using bicycles and subway trains for
 their daily travel instead of cars, which has so far helped reduce a tremendous amount
 of CO2 released into the air, and improved air quality. Second, the problem of polluted
 oceans has also been tackled in many places thanks to groups of young people who
 voluntarily spend their time cleaning up beaches, or even diving into water to pick up
 trash. For example, many students in Nha Trang, a coastal city of Vietnam, spent nearly
 their whole summer holiday in 2018 keeping the beaches of their hometown clean by
 collecting all the trash from tourists, and even banned Chinese people from entering
 certain areas to prevent them from littering.
 In conclusion, I hold the view that politicians alone cannot deal with all environmental
 problems, and therefore individuals should also make a contribution to protecting our
 environment.

question: government only provide primary education and no secondary education. What do you think?
essay:
It is believed that countries which have high unemployment rates should only offer primary education; and
 extending secondary education to students who are not likely to find work in the future is a hopeless attempt.
 I personally consider this to be a flawed argument, and I will explain my reasoning in the essay below.
 Important as it is to a child’s overall development, primary education does not provide any necessary work skills. The
 fundamental aim of this early stage of education is simply to lay a basis for a student’s cognitive development through
 the learning of language, art and basic science. People who complete this level of education are not equipped with
 the necessary skills and knowledge to enter the workforce. While those people may find blue-collar employment, it
 is unlikely that they can get a decent job. Therefore, if a country with a high unemployment level only offers primary
 education to the majority of students, the outcome will not be worthwhile.
 Furthermore, I would argue that investing in secondary schooling systems and delivering secondary education
 to young students are of greater importance when it comes to improving the overall employment rate of
 a country. In fact, nowadays, many middle and high schools hire a large number of secondary education
 degree holders who work better with younger age groups and are more familiar with the subjects taught at
 this level of education. There are also many other types of non-academic careers, such as in the beauty and
 entertainment industries, that only require a secondary education degree. Last but not least, I believe that
 we should not restrict secondary education to a certain group of students who are deemed eligible because
 everyone deserves an equal opportunity to access education.
 In conclusion, I disagree that providing only primary education is the best course of action to be adopted in
 the context of a nation with a high rate of unemployment. Secondary education, on the other hand, is a major
 contributor to combating unemployment. I also believe that we should not dispense secondary education to
 only a select number of students as everyone has the right to be educated.

question: governments rather than private com panies. Do you agree or disagree?
essay:
Scientific research plays an important role in the development of humanity. Some people think that the responsibility
 for conducting and managing it should be taken by governments, and not by private companies. Personally, I believe
 that both governments and private businesses should have the right and duty to carry out research.
 I believe that governments should play the leading role in performing research due to a number of reasons.
 Firstly, scientific research is the key to finding solutions to the pressing concerns of society. Thorough medical
 research, for example, needs to be conducted by governments to develop vaccines against potential epidemics.
 Research into renewable energies is also crucial to deal with global warming. These kinds of research require a
 great deal of spending, which can only be afforded by the government. In addition, scientific studies controlled
 by governments will ensure that reliable and ethical methods are used, and minimize the risk of these studies
 being used for criminal activity. For instance, studies related to nuclear power or weapons must be carried out
 or supervised by governmental organizations to protect a country from terrorism or other warfare.
 However, private companies can do research which is not carried out by governments. In fact, many useful
 scientific discoveries have been made by private institutions. To illustrate, the invention of the iPhone by
 Apple, a corporation in the US began the era of smartphones and facilitated modern forms of interaction
 between people all over the world. Furthermore, encouraging profit-driven companies to conduct scientific
 investigations will help them develop their own products and create competition in the marketplace. Society
 will therefore benefit from new inventions and better products.
 In conclusion, although governments should control and carry out major scientific studies, private companies
 should not be deprived of the right to do research.

question: In many cities, there is little control on the design and construction of new houses, so people can build houses in their own styles rather than building them with the same style as the old houses in the local area. Do the advantages outweigh the disadvantages?
essay:
In many cities around the world, people have little influence in the design and construction
 of their houses. Many people believe it is better to allow people to freely choose the
 style and construction of their new homes rather than forcing everyone to follow the
 uniform style of the local area. In my opinion, a diversity in housing styles can bring
 many more benefits than drawbacks to an area.
 To begin with, cities in Vietnam, such as Saigon, have very few, or even no, regulations
 on local housing styles, which means that residents in these areas are free to decide
 on the architecture for their home. The only disadvantage that may arise is the lack
 of uniformity of houses, which can be a unique feature in certain places. The houses in
 Hoi An, for instance, demonstrate a consistent style and therefore have made the city a
 popular tourist destination. If the house owners in this city change the style and break
 this uniformity, the number of tourists visiting the area is likely to decrease.
 However, the advantages brought about by a diversity in housing styles are more worthy
 of consideration. Firstly, buildings reflecting a variety of architectural designs can make
 a city more attractive and appealing to people of different cultures. For example, the
 buildings in the centre of Ho Chi Minh city are designed in both European and Asian
 styles, which has eventually given the city a more cosmopolitan atmosphere, with
 residents coming from many different countries. Secondly, the freedom of choice
 in housing styles can allow local people to be more creative, as they can design their
 homes in a unique way.
 In conclusion, though potentially making some areas less attractive by losing a part of
 their character, a diversity in architectural styles should be widely encouraged, as it can
 beautify a city and allow residents to be more creative.

question: In many countries women and men are working full time. It is therefore, logical for women and men to share household works. To what extent do you agree or disagree?
essay:
It is becoming a trend now that husbands and wives should handle the household works together since both members of the family are working full time. In my opinion, it is convincing that the house tasks should be equally spread between both genders for the following reasons.
 In many developing countries, people are required to work more than before; as a result, both genders are having busy lives nowadays and cannot handle the works of the house by only one of them. If the chores are being handled by both men and women, it increases the other partner's satisfaction and shows responsibilty. For instance, if a wife has several tasks in the kitchen to finish before heading to work and her husband helped their children to get ready for school, this emotionally affects the wife and she will eventually feel gratification.
 Secondly, as technology evolves, people are having a hard time to manage the tasks of their homes and might eventually hire a nanny to take care of the children and home. As a result, children would feel loneliness in addition to being ignored by their parents. Although it is not easy to manage the work life and family at the same time, giving priority to one before the other can be counter-productive. Finally, as both partners work together to handle household work, it will eventually lead to a balanced life, their daily schedules will be organised; moreover, they will have more time to spend with their children thus, the home will not have problems as long as the mother and the father are working together to manage their home.
 By way of conclusion, I would like to re-affirm there is a vital role for both males and females in order to keep their homes well balanced. Despite having a full employement job for both of them, it is necessary to share the works of their home together.

question:
In many countries, people are spending more hours at their workplace. Why is that? Is that anegative
 and positive developement?
essay:
Spending extra hours at work is becoming more common in
 recent years. This phenomenon may stem from people’s need
 for higher wage and their increasing workload, and I firmly
 believe that this trend is bringing more cons than pros.
 Nowadays, a lot of people are overworking due to the pressure
 from daily necessities and work. In many cities, due to the
 escalating living cost(1), people are made to work extra hours to
 afford lives. In other words, overworking now is not an option
 but rather an obligation for many people to survive. In some
 other cases, people choose to work overtime to cope with their
 increasing workload. In the modern age, as competition at work
 is becoming more intense than ever, many people feel an urge
 to push themselves harder and work longer hours to finish their
 work, believing that taking on more responsibilities(2) can help
 them and their organizations thrive. 
 Spending extra hours at work is becoming more common in
 recent years. This phenomenon may stem from people’s need
 for higher wage and their increasing workload, and I firmly
 believe that this trend is bringing more cons than pros.
 Nowadays, a lot of people are overworking due to the pressure
 from daily necessities and work. In many cities, due to the
 escalating living cost(1), people are made to work extra hours to
 afford lives. In other words, overworking now is not an option
 but rather an obligation for many people to survive. In some
 other cases, people choose to work overtime to cope with their
 increasing workload. In the modern age, as competition at work
 is becoming more intense than ever, many people feel an urge
 to push themselves harder and work longer hours to finish their
 work, believing that taking on more responsibilities(2) can help
 them and their organizations thrive.

question: In many countries, people like to eat a wider range of food that cannot be grown in their local place. Therefore, much of the food people eat today has to come from other regions. Do you think the advantages of this development outweigh disadvantages?
essay:
These days, it is quite common for people to eat foods that are not, or cannot, be grown
 or produced where they live. As a consequence, large quantities of food are either
 transported long distances within a country or imported from other countries. This
 essay will review some of the advantages and disadvantages of this trend.
 Thanks to globalisation, the increase in efficient transportation between and within
 countries, and a growing trend in food culture, people can now enjoy a vast array of
 foods like never before. This trend has many advantages. Firstly, having access to a
 wide variety of foods from different regions can provide people with a highly nutritious
 diet that can help to promote health and prevent disease. For example, some kinds
 of nutrient-rich berries that can only be grown in Asia can now be enjoyed by people
 all over the world. Furthermore, with such a growing interest in exotic foods and food
 culture, people can experience a range of cuisine without leaving their own town or city.
 However, this trend also comes with a number of disadvantages. For example, some
 communities around the world where exotic and rare foods are grown are losing
 their own access to their food sources. When demand for a certain food increases,
 the price also increases, and people in poorer countries where the food is grown can
 sometimes not afford to buy it anymore. Another problem with this trend is that the
 constant transportation of foods over long distances contributes to climate change
 due to the large amount of carbon emissions produced by transportation. And lastly,
 many foods that are transported long distances need to be treated with chemicals and
 preservatives which make them less healthy to consume.
 Overall, although there are many benefits to having access to a wide variety of foods,
 personally, I believe that the disadvantages of this trend outweigh the advantages.

question: In many countries, the costs of living are rising. What are the effects of this on individuals and society? Suggest solutions to this problem.
essay:
These days, many people around the world are struggling to deal with rising living costs.
 The following essay will outline some of the effects of this issue, and some possible
 solutions.
 Higher living costs are affecting both individuals and society in a number of ways. On
 an individual level, many people, particularly those living in big cities these days, cannot
 keep up with the rising costs of living and therefore have to have more than one job in
 order to make ends meet. As a result, these people barely have any time left for leisure
 activities and could even be more prone to negative medical conditions like depression
 or anxiety disorder. On a societal level, rising living costs can cause people with financial
 problems to turn to crime in order to make more money. For example, in some Asian
 countries where the monthly salary of a police officer is very low, many of them turn to
 corruption to make enough money.
 Although rising living costs pose serious problems in many places, there are a number of
 solutions that could be implemented to help tackle this issue. Firstly, individuals need to
 learn how to budget their income more effectively, and be careful not to waste money.
 By adhering to a strict budget, people will be able to pay their bills and save money to
 cover other expenses. On the other hand, governments should enact laws to ensure
 that citizens receive minimum wages that are enough to cover rising living expenses.
 Lastly, governments can create more jobs, particularly in big cities where living costs are
 much higher.
 In conclusion, although many people around the world are finding it difficult to cope
 with rising living costs, there are a number of solutions that may help to mitigate the
 problem.

question: In many nations, people in huge cities either live alone or in small family units, instead of in large, extended family groups. Is this a positive or negative trend?
essay:
In many large cities around the world, it has become quite common for people to either
 live alone or in a small family, as opposed to living in large extended family households.
 This essay will take a closer look at some of the positive and negative aspects that arise
 with this trend.
 On the one hand, this trend does have some positive aspects. For example, different
 generations want and value different things these days. Many young people do not
 wish to live the traditional lifestyles of their parents and grandparents. Therefore, living
 alone allows them the freedom to do as they please without having to face constant
 judgement or criticism. Furthermore, many older people do not wish to live the hectic,
 fast-paced lifestyle of modern city life and prefer to live a slower-paced, healthier life in
 the countryside during their retirement. Living alone, or in smaller family groups allows
 each generation to live their preferred lifestyle.
 However, this trend also brings several drawbacks. Firstly, as parents and grandparents
 get older, they may face a number of physical and mental health problems, and by living
 alone, they may lack the care and attention they need from their children. Secondly,
 children will have less interaction and influence from grandparents, and potentially
 miss out on important life lessons that can be taught from grandparents who have a
 lot of valuable life experience. Important traditions and customs may also be lost from
 generation to generation. And finally, living alone, or in small family groups can lead to
 poor family bonds between generations.
 In conclusion, I believe that while this trend does bring some benefits, overall they are
 definitely outweighed by the drawbacks.

question:
In most countries, fast food is becoming cheaper and more available. Do you think the advantages
 of this development outweigh the disadvantages?
essay:
In many countries these days, fast food is becoming more
 affordable and more readily-available(1). In my opinion, the
 disadvantages of this situation far outweigh any possible
 advantages, and I believe that people need to become more aware
 about the negative impacts that fast food has on their health.
 The first main advantage of fast food is that it is convenient. Most
 people who buy fast food buy it because it is far more convenient
 than buying ingredients at the market and taking them home to
 cook. These days, many students and people who work full time
 are far too busy(2) and tired to be bothered to cook a meal(3) after
 a long day of work or study. It is far more convenient(2) to just
 stop and pick up some fast food(5) on their way home from work
 or school. However, fast food is usually extremely unhealthy(6)
 and can lead to many serious health concerns(7) such as obesity,
 diabetes, and heart disease(8), amongst others. Therefore, fast
 food is convenient in the short term(9), but very inconvenient
 in the long term(9) when you develop a chronic illness(10) from
 excessive fast food intake.
 The second advantage of fast food is the price. Fast food is
 usually relatively cheap, which makes it a very easy choice for
 many people on a low budget(11), such as students and young
 people. However, although someone may save a small amount of
 money in the short term by purchasing cheap fast food, their bank
 balance will suffer the consequences(12) when they are forced
 to pay large medical bills(13) in years to come after developing
 some form of chronic illness. Chronic illness due to unhealthy
 diets and lifestyles puts a massive burden on(14) the national
 healthcare system and budget, which results in a negative effect
 on a country’s economy and ends up impacting all the citizens
 indirectly.
 In conclusion, the convenience and low cost of fast food will
 certainly have a negative effect on the long term health and
 economy of a country and governments need to educate their
 people about such issues.

question: In most successful companies, some people think that communication between employers and workers is the most important factor. Other people say that other factors are more important. Discuss both views and give your opinion.
essay:
Some people think that effective communication between managers and subordinate
 employees is the most important feature of an organization’s success. However, there
 are other contributing factors to an organization’s success that may be more important.
 I will discuss both sides of this argument in this essay.
 Managers make decisions about the direction of an organization and employees
 implement those decisions. Consequently, it is important that, when a decision is
 made, this decision can be communicated effectively to the workers. If the workers
 misinterpret a decision, the consequences could range from a minor discrepancy, to a
 serious setback for the organization. Effective communication and implementation of
 decisions will result in the organization running as the managers want it to be run.
 There are many other factors that contribute to a company’s success, some of which
 could be considered more important than effective communication. For example, the
 competency of managers themselves contributes significantly to success. It does not
 matter if a worker can effectively employ a policy if the policy, created by the manager,
 is useless or detrimental to the organization’s success. Another contributing factor to
 success is the reliability and availability of good workers. An organization could have
 the best managers in the world, but if there is not a skilled and reliable work force
 available to them, progress will be slow and success difficult to come by. I believe that
 both of these factors are more important than communication between managers and
 workers itself.
 Communication between managers and workers is an important factor in an organization’s
 success. However, in order for communication to be effective, an organization needs
 competent managers and a reliable, skilled workforce.

question: In recent years, the family structure has changed, as well as family roles. What are the changes occurring? Do you think these changes are positive or negative?
essay:
In recent times, there has been a number of changes within the structure of families, and the roles that family
 members play. This essay will discuss those changes, and the impacts these trends are having on families.
 Firstly, in a lot of countries, many people are now choosing to live in a nuclear family structure, where only
 parents and children live together, and grandparents live separately. In the past, however, it was quite common
 in many countries for three, or even four generations of a family to all live under the one roof. There has also
 been a shift in the roles that various family members play. For example, in the past, it was commonplace for men
 to have to work to provide for their families, and women to stay at home to cook, clean, and raise the children.
 However, many women nowadays are choosing to work instead, while some fathers stay at home to help out
 with family and household duties.
 These changes can bring about both positive and negative effects. Nuclear family structures allow more freedom to
 parents who no longer want to live in their hometown, in order to pursue their career. Many people need to live in
 large cities these days to find work, however many older, retired people want to remain living in their hometown.
 Though, one disadvantage of the nuclear family is that grandparents may not be available to help raise and care for
 grandchildren. In terms of the changing roles, women have greatly benefited from the opportunity to pursue a career,
 and become the breadwinner of the family, instead of being a housewife. One downside of this trend though might
 be that some people would argue that women are better at domestic duties than stay-at-home dads.
 In conclusion, I believe there are both positive and negative effects from these changes, though the impacts
 on each individual family will be different.

question: In some countries young people are encouraged to work or travel for a year between finishing high school and starting university studies. Discuss the advantages and disadvantages for young people who decide to do this .
essay:
Young people in some countries are encouraged to take one gap year to work or travel abroad between their high school and university studies. This essay will discuss both of the benefits and drawbacks of the experiences of one year adventure in foreign countries for young people.
 To start with, there are several advantages to this adventure abroad. Firstly, young people can meet people from different cultures, beliefs, and ages, and broaden their thoughts and experiences of the world. For instance, they may have cultural shock and reflect their own views and values about life and the world. In this way, they may become more open-minded and tolerant of different people and cultures. Secondly, they can make friends with people in other countries and broaden their social networks. This can become long-term friendships and they can gain a global perspective of the world. This is not only beneficial for themselves, but it can also benefit their societies and countries in long run as well.
 However, there are also potential drawbacks to this journey. First and foremost, they can encounter some dangerous situations, such as being scammed, robbed, and even physically harmed. Numerous reports in the media about unfortunate cases that young people in foreign countries being kidnapped, forced to consume or sell drugs, and even being killed. It could be dangerous for them if they meet local gangster bands and have no one to rescue them. Therefore, it is essential that young people take precautions to keep their safety while abroad.
